html:
[616]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 且异境多在人踪罕至之处，轿与船不能到也 - Phrase meaning 'Moreover, the exotic places are often in remote areas where carriages and boats cannot reach.' The word '且' (qiě) adds an additional point, and '异境' (yìjìng) refers to exotic or unusual places.
  • 冠山为寺 - Phrase meaning 'crowning the mountain with a temple.' It uses the metaphor of crowning to describe the prominent position of the temple on the mountain.
  • 架木作亭 - Phrase meaning 'building a pavilion by constructing wooden structures.' It describes the construction of a pavilion using wood.
  • 楼台烟雨 - Phrase meaning 'towering buildings amidst misty rain.' It evokes a picturesque scene of buildings shrouded in mist and rain.
  • 绮丽清幽 - Phrase meaning 'gorgeous and serene.' It describes a beautiful and tranquil environment.
  • 过松岭，渡长桥 - Phrases meaning 'crossing the pine ridge and crossing the long bridge.' They describe the journey through specific geographical features.
  • 南屏之山，怪石攒列 - Phrase meaning 'the mountains of Nanping are lined with strange rocks.' It describes the unique geological formations in the Nanping area.
  • 下有古寺 - Phrase meaning 'below there is an ancient temple.' It indicates the presence of an old temple at the base of the mountains.
  • 北曰雷峰，有塔高而色紫 - Phrase meaning 'to the north is Leifeng, which has a tall purple tower.' It describes the location and appearance of the Leifeng Pagoda.
  • 西曰苏堤，从南抵北，作六桥以通舟，植梅柳于其上 - Phrase meaning 'to the west is the Su Causeway, which runs from south to north, with six bridges built to connect boats, and willows and plum trees planted along it.' It describes the layout and features of the Su Causeway.
  • 堤西有园亭，引湖为沼以蓄鱼 - Phrase meaning 'west of the causeway, there are garden pavilions, with a pond created by diverting part of the lake to house fish.' It describes the garden pavilions and the pond.
  • 堤东有洲，旁有三塔，影入洲中 - Phrase meaning 'east of the causeway, there is a small island, with three towers nearby, their shadows reflected in the island.' It describes the small island and the towers.
  • 潭北有亭，翼然水面者，湖心亭也 - Phrase meaning 'north of the pond, there is a pavilion, gracefully overhanging the water, known as the Lake Heart Pavilion.' It describes the Lake Heart Pavilion.
  • 亭北突起而韶秀者，孤山也 - Phrase meaning 'north of the pavilion, there rises a solitary and beautiful mountain, known as Solitary Hill.' It describes Solitary Hill.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 西湖 - Refers to West Lake, one of the most famous scenic spots in Hangzhou, China. It is renowned for its natural beauty and cultural significance.
  • 南屏晚钟 - One of the Ten Views of West Lake, referring to the sound of the evening bell from the ancient temple on Nanping Mountain.
  • 雷峰夕照 - One of the Ten Views of West Lake, referring to the sunset view of Leifeng Pagoda.
  • 苏堤春晓 - One of the Ten Views of West Lake, referring to the early morning view of the Su Causeway in spring.
  • 花港观鱼 - One of the Ten Views of West Lake, referring to the fish-watching scene in the Flower Harbor.
  • 三潭印月 - One of the Ten Views of West Lake, referring to the reflection of the moon in the Three Ponds.
  • 湖心亭 - One of the Ten Views of West Lake, referring to the Lake Heart Pavilion, a famous pavilion on West Lake.
  • 孤山 - One of the Ten Views of West Lake, referring to Solitary Hill, a small hill in the middle of West Lake.

Proper Nouns

  • 西湖 - West Lake, a famous scenic spot in Hangzhou, China.
  • 南屏 - Nanping, a mountain area near West Lake.
  • 雷峰 - Leifeng, a pagoda located on the north side of West Lake.
  • 苏堤 - Su Causeway, a famous causeway in West Lake named after Su Shi.
  • 花港 - Flower Harbor, a scenic area in West Lake.
  • 三潭 - Three Ponds, a scenic area in West Lake.
  • 湖心亭 - Lake Heart Pavilion, a famous pavilion on West Lake.
  • 孤山 - Solitary Hill, a small hill in the middle of West Lake.

[617]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 山有紫垣缭绕者 - Descriptive phrase meaning 'a mountain surrounded by purple walls,' which is a metaphor for a palace or imperial residence.
  • 其东直抵杭城者 - Descriptive phrase meaning 'extending directly to Hangzhou City.'
  • 苏堤纵而白堤横 - Parallel structure describing the orientation of the Su Causeway and the Bai Causeway.
  • 孤山介两堤之间 - Describes the position of Solitary Mountain between the two causeways.
  • 尝读史至国家兴亡之际 - Describes the act of reading history up to the time of a nation's rise and fall.
  • 不能无疑于天也 - Expresses doubt about heaven.
  • 当武穆提兵北伐 - Describes Yue Fei leading troops north to attack.
  • 山东、河朔，豪杰响应 - Describes heroes responding in the regions of Shandong and the northern plains.
  • 韩常内附 - Describes General Han Chang submitting to Yue Fei's forces.
  • 兀术外奔 - Describes Wujie fleeing during Yue Fei's campaign.
  • 使其予秦桧以暴疾 - Describes a hypothetical scenario where Qin Hui is given a sudden illness.
  • 假武穆以遐年 - Describes a hypothetical scenario where Yue Fei is granted a long life.
  • 复神州而返二圣 - Describes restoring the Central Plains and returning the two emperors.
  • 至易易耳 - Describes something as very easy.
  • 而顾不然 - Describes the actual outcome as not being so.
  • 待其人之云亡 - Describes waiting for someone's death.
  • 邦国殄瘁 - Describes the state being ruined.
  • 易代而后 - Describes after a change of dynasties.
  • 乃复祀武穆而击桧 - Describes Yue Fei being worshipped again and Qin Hui being struck.
  • 岂天心悔过 - Expresses doubt about heaven regretting its mistake.
  • 而假手于人以盖前愆 - Describes using people to cover up past wrongs.
  • 抑天终不悔 - Expresses doubt about heaven never regretting.
  • 而人奋其力与天争耶 - Describes people striving with all their might to compete with heaven.
  • 善恶之报 - Describes rewards and punishments for good and evil.
  • 不于其身，必于其子孙 - Describes that rewards and punishments are not in one's own life but in their descendants.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 行宫 - Imperial residence, often used by emperors when they were away from the capital.
  • 白堤 - The Bai Causeway, a famous dike in Hangzhou, built by Bai Juyi during his tenure as governor of Hangzhou.
  • 苏堤 - The Su Causeway, another famous dike in Hangzhou, built by Su Shi during his tenure as governor of Hangzhou.
  • 孤山 - A mountain in Hangzhou, known for its scenic beauty and cultural significance.
  • 岳武穆庙 - The temple dedicated to Yue Fei, a national hero and military general who was unjustly executed by Qin Hui.
  • 秦桧夫妇 - Qin Hui and his wife, who were notorious for their treachery and betrayal of Yue Fei.
  • 山东、河朔 - Refers to the regions of Shandong and the northern plains, areas where Yue Fei's northern campaign was successful.
  • 韩常 - A general who submitted to Yue Fei's forces.
  • 兀术 - Wujie, the leader of the Jurchen forces who fled during Yue Fei's campaign.
  • 二圣 - The two emperors who were captured by the Jurchens and later returned to China.

Proper Nouns

  • 行宫 - Imperial residence.
  • 白堤 - Bai Causeway.
  • 苏堤 - Su Causeway.
  • 孤山 - Solitary Mountain.
  • 岳武穆庙 - Temple of Yue Fei.
  • 秦桧夫妇 - Qin Hui and his wife.
  • 山东 - Shandong region.
  • 河朔 - Northern plains.
  • 韩常 - General Han Chang.
  • 兀术 - Wujie.
  • 二圣 - The two emperors.

[618]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 今闻秦氏盛而岳氏式微 - Classical expression meaning 'I have heard that the Qin family is thriving while the Yue family is declining.'
  • 人之好恶之心，何由而生也 - Philosophical question meaning 'How does the heart's inclination towards good and evil arise?'
  • 天之好恶，既与人同 - Statement meaning 'The heavens' inclination towards good and evil is the same as that of humans.'
  • 胡为误于其身，复误于其子孙，而终不悔耶 - Rhetorical question meaning 'Why do they err in their own lives and then pass on this error to their descendants, yet never repent?'
  • 此其故圣人知之矣 - Statement meaning 'This reason is known by the sages.'
  • 运之有否泰，数也 - Statement meaning 'The alternation between adversity and prosperity is a matter of fate.'
  • 天之所不能违也 - Statement meaning 'It is something that heaven cannot violate.'
  • 非小人得志而害君子，则运不成 - Conditional statement meaning 'If the wicked do not achieve power and harm the virtuous, then the cycle of fate will not be complete.'
  • 万世之人心，好君子而恶小人者，天之理之常 - Statement meaning 'Throughout all time, people's hearts incline towards the virtuous and away from the wicked, which is a constant law of heaven.'
  • 一时之气运，福小人而祸君子者，天之数之变 - Statement meaning 'In a given era, the prevailing trends may bring fortune to the wicked and misfortune to the virtuous, which is a change in the course of fate.'
  • 万物之于天，犹子之于父，臣之于君也 - Metaphorical statement meaning 'All things in relation to heaven are like children in relation to their fathers and subjects in relation to their rulers.'
  • 龙逄、比干，其君不以为忠 - Historical reference meaning 'Lóng Páng and Bǐ Gān, their rulers did not consider them loyal.'
  • 申生、伯奇，其父不以为孝 - Historical reference meaning 'Shēn Shēng and Bó Qí, their fathers did not consider them filial.'
  • 孝子不敢非其亲，忠臣不敢怼其君 - Statement meaning 'Filial sons dare not criticize their parents, and loyal ministers dare not blame their rulers.'
  • 而于天又何怨焉 - Rhetorical question meaning 'And what complaint can they have against heaven?'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 秦氏 - Refers to the Qin family, likely a prominent family in the context.
  • 岳氏 - Refers to the Yue family, likely a prominent family in the context.
  • 龙逄 - Historical figure known for his loyalty to his ruler.
  • 比干 - Historical figure known for his loyalty to his ruler.
  • 申生 - Historical figure known for his filial piety.
  • 伯奇 - Historical figure known for his filial piety.
  • 《易》 - The Book of Changes, one of the Five Classics.
  • 泰 - Term from the Book of Changes meaning prosperity.
  • 否 - Term from the Book of Changes meaning adversity.
  • 圣人 - Refers to sages or wise men, often associated with Confucian thought.

Proper Nouns

  • 秦氏 - The Qin family.
  • 岳氏 - The Yue family.
  • 龙逄 - Lóng Páng.
  • 比干 - Bǐ Gān.
  • 申生 - Shēn Shēng.
  • 伯奇 - Bó Qí.
  • 《易》 - The Book of Changes.
  • 泰 - Tài (prosperity).
  • 否 - Fǒu (adversity).
  • 圣人 - Sages or wise men.

[619]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 庙西有坟 - Indicates the location of the tomb, with '庙西' meaning 'west of the temple.'
  • 内有二冢 - Refers to two tombs inside the tomb complex.
  • 武穆王与其子云也 - Identifies the tomb's occupants as the Wumu Wang (a title) and his son, Yún (a name).
  • 坟南亭台，临湖结构 - Describes the southern part of the tomb area, which has pavilions and buildings constructed near the lake.
  • 朱栏碧槛 - Refers to red railings and green balustrades, typical decorative elements in traditional Chinese architecture.
  • 与绿水红莲相掩映 - Describes the beautiful scene where the green water and red lotus flowers reflect each other.
  • 所谓“曲院风荷”也 - Indicates that this place is known as 'Quyuan Fenghe' (a scenic spot), a famous attraction.
  • 初在南屏望湖 - Refers to the initial view of the lake from the southern screen (a scenic viewpoint).
  • 路人指曰 - Means 'a passerby pointed out,' indicating a guide or local's explanation.
  • 高而顶有塔者，南高峰也 - Describes the Southern High Peak, noting its height and the tower at the top.
  • 其遥与高同者，北高峰也 - Describes the Northern High Peak, noting its distance and height similar to the Southern High Peak.
  • 兹由岳坟而西 - Indicates the current direction of travel, moving west from the Yue Mausoleum.
  • 路旁皆山，苍松翠柏，蔽岫连云 - Describes the scenery along the road, with mountains covered in dark green pines and cypresses, reaching up to the clouds.
  • 林中徐步 - Means 'walking slowly in the forest,' indicating a leisurely pace.
  • 忽见清溪 - Means 'suddenly seeing a clear stream,' indicating an unexpected discovery.
  • 白石磷磷，落花沉涧 - Describes the clear stream with white stones visible and fallen flowers floating in the stream.
  • 鸟语如簧 - Describes the birds' chirping as sounding like a musical instrument.
  • 心中恍惚 - Means 'feeling dazed or confused,' indicating a moment of uncertainty.
  • 冀有所遇 - Means 'hoping to encounter something,' indicating a desire for a discovery.
  • 沿山深入 - Means 'following the mountain path deeper,' indicating further exploration.
  • 见一村落 - Means 'seeing a village,' indicating the discovery of a settlement.
  • 酒帘树间，茶棚竹下 - Describes the village with wine banners hanging among trees and tea pavilions under bamboo.
  • 路西有坊，题曰“飞来峰” - Indicates a gate on the west side of the road, inscribed with the name 'Feilai Peak.'
  • 过坊而西，乃见奇峰特峙，流水环周 - Describes the scene after passing through the gate, with a unique peak standing out and a stream surrounding it.
  • 洞在山腹，桥当洞口 - Describes a cave in the mountain body and a bridge at the cave entrance.
  • 渡桥入洞 - Means 'crossing the bridge into the cave,' indicating entering the cave.
  • 岩壑空幻，石骨玲珑 - Describes the cave's interior as spacious and mysterious, with delicate rock formations.
  • 乳泉滴沥，积而成池 - Describes the cave's stalactites dripping water, forming a pool.
  • 洞顶怪石，如古树倒垂，云霞横出 - Describes the strange rocks on the cave ceiling, resembling ancient trees hanging down and clouds and mist emerging.
  • 孔穴贯串，八达四通 - Describes the cave's passages as interconnected, leading in all directions.
  • 或巨或细，或暗或明 - Describes the cave's varying sizes and lighting conditions.
  • 出洞西行 - Means 'leaving the cave and heading west,' indicating continued travel.
  • 溪边岩下，石皆奇秀 - Describes the stones along the stream and under the rocks as uniquely beautiful.
  • 卓立林间者，往往与松竹争长 - Describes the stones standing tall in the forest, often competing with pines and bamboos in height.
  • 山侧有放生池 - Describes a pond for releasing animals on the side of the mountain.
  • 池上有冷泉亭 - Describes a pavilion named 'Lengquan Pavilion' (Cold Spring Pavilion) above the pond.
  • 高峰插天，修篁蔽日 - Describes the tall peaks reaching into the sky and the tall bamboo shading the sun.
  • 流泉清池，环亭左右 - Describes the clear stream and pond surrounding the pavilion.
  • 盛夏正午，冷若深秋 - Describes the coolness of the pavilion during midsummer, feeling like early autumn.
  • 亭北有寺，扁曰“云林” - Describes a temple to the north of the pavilion, with the name 'Yunlin Temple' (Cloud Forest Temple) written on it.
  • 未暇入也 - Means 'not having time to enter,' indicating a decision to continue without visiting the temple.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 武穆王 - Refers to Yue Fei, a famous general and national hero during the Song Dynasty, posthumously honored as Wumu Wang (Wumu Duke).
  • 岳坟 - Refers to the tomb of Yue Fei, located in Hangzhou, Zhejiang Province.
  • 曲院风荷 - A famous scenic spot in Hangzhou, known for its beautiful lotus flowers and pavilions.
  • 南屏 - Refers to Nanping Mountain, a scenic area near West Lake in Hangzhou.
  • 南高峰 - Refers to the Southern High Peak, one of the mountains near West Lake.
  • 北高峰 - Refers to the Northern High Peak, another mountain near West Lake.
  • 飞来峰 - Refers to Feilai Peak, a famous scenic spot in Hangzhou, known for its unique rock formations and caves.
  • 冷泉亭 - Refers to Lengquan Pavilion, a famous pavilion in Hangzhou, known for its coolness even in summer.

[620]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 過寺而西 - Directional phrase meaning 'after passing the temple, going west.'
  • 小園别墅 - Refers to a small garden villa or a secluded retreat.
  • 布置佳勝 - Means 'arranged in a beautiful and advantageous manner.'
  • 縱目流覽 - Means 'to gaze and stroll leisurely.'
  • 忘其路之遠近 - Means 'forgetting the distance of the path.'
  • 幽林密箐 - Describes a dense, secluded forest.
  • 曲折其中 - Means 'winding through it.'
  • 仰望，不見天日 - Means 'looking up and not seeing the sky or sun.'
  • 心中驚疑 - Means 'feeling surprised and doubtful in one's heart.'
  • 誤入何境 - Means 'mistakenly entering what kind of place.'
  • 一借問 - Means 'to ask once.'
  • 深山無人 - Means 'deep in the mountains, there is no one.'
  • 林間企望 - Means 'looking forward in the forest.'
  • 度岭而去 - Means 'crossing the ridge and going away.'
  • 天風南來 - Means 'the wind from the south.'
  • 微聞鼓樂之聲 - Means 'barely hearing the sound of drums and music.'
  • 見此乃悟身在天竺峰也 - Means 'seeing this, one realizes they are on Tianzhu Peak.'
  • 日將暮 - Means 'the day is nearing its end.'
  • 四圍幽壑深林 - Means 'surrounded by deep ravines and dense forests.'
  • 不類人境 - Means 'not resembling a human dwelling place.'
  • 懼其為虎豹之窟穴 - Means 'fearing it to be a lair of tigers and leopards.'
  • 山魑木魅所往來 - Means 'a place where mountain demons and tree spirits come and go.'
  • 因返 - Means 'therefore, returned.'
  • 尋前所見村落而歇焉 - Means 'found the village seen before and rested there.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 天竺 - Refers to Tianzhu, a mountain in Hangzhou, Zhejiang Province, known for its Buddhist temples.
  • 回禄 - A euphemism for fire, often used in classical texts to describe a conflagration.
  • 天竺峰 - Refers to a specific peak on Tianzhu Mountain.
  • 鼓樂之聲 - Refers to the sounds of drums and music, often associated with religious or festive occasions.

Proper Nouns

  • 天竺 - The name of the mountain.
  • 天竺峰 - The specific peak on Tianzhu Mountain.
  • 飛來峰 - Another peak on Tianzhu Mountain, known for its scenic beauty.

[621]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 次早 - Means 'the next morning,' indicating a specific time of day.
  • 不入洞而登其颠 - A concise way of saying 'without entering the cave, he climbed to the top,' emphasizing the action of climbing.
  • 远望旭日出海，江潮涌金，晓雾成霞，山岚抹黛 - A vivid description of the scenery, using classical imagery to convey the beauty of the scene.
  • 景色变幻，林密怪奇 - Describes the changing scenery and the dense, strange forest, using poetic language.
  • 自疑此身或恐飞去 - A metaphorical expression meaning 'he felt as if his body might fly away,' conveying a sense of awe or wonder.
  • 昔韩世忠忤秦桧，解官携酒，日游西湖，建翠微亭于飞来峰上 - A historical allusion to General Han Shizhong's defiance of Qin Hui, his resignation, and his daily visits to West Lake, where he built the Cuiwei Pavilion on Feilai Peak.
  • 唯斯人也，而后称斯山也 - A rhetorical statement meaning 'only such a person could truly appreciate this mountain,' emphasizing the connection between the person and the place.
  • 时值四月八日，寺于此日斋僧 - Indicates that it was on the fourth day of the eighth month, when the temple held a vegetarian meal for monks.
  • 本寺住持，披法衣上堂讲经 - Describes the abbot of the temple, who was dressed in monastic robes and delivering a sermon.
  • 其大和尚曰帝辉，年可九十余矣，巍然据高座 - Introduces the abbot, named Dihui, who is about ninety years old, and describes his dignified posture on the high seat.
  • 首座二人，侍者八人，其下行列而拜跪者，可三百众 - Describes the two chief monks and eight attendants, and the monks and nuns who were lined up below, numbering about three hundred.
  • 比丘与比丘尼咸在，其威仪俯仰皆娴谨 - Indicates that both monks and nuns were present, and describes their respectful and disciplined behavior.
  • 独惜所讲无所发明，即成书而诵之 - A critical statement meaning 'but the sermon was unoriginal, as if it were just a recitation of a book.'
  • 其下不必尽闻，闻者不必尽解 - Indicates that the audience did not fully hear or understand the sermon.
  • 徒听侍者拜云则拜，起云则起而已 - Describes the mechanical behavior of the monks and nuns, who only followed the movements of the attendants.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 韩世忠忤秦桧 - Refers to General Han Shizhong's defiance of Qin Hui, a historical figure known for his political intrigues.
  • 解官携酒，日游西湖 - Describes Han Shizhong's resignation and his daily visits to West Lake, a famous scenic area in Hangzhou.
  • 建翠微亭于飞来峰上 - Refers to the construction of the Cuiwei Pavilion on Feilai Peak, a famous landmark in Hangzhou.
  • 云林 - Refers to the temple's name, which is also known as 'Cloud Forest Temple.'
  • 四月八日 - Refers to the fourth day of the eighth month, a traditional Chinese festival known as the Buddha's Birthday.
  • 斋僧 - Refers to the practice of offering vegetarian meals to monks, a common Buddhist tradition.
  • 住持 - Refers to the abbot of the temple, the highest-ranking monk.
  • 大和尚 - Refers to the abbot, a title of respect.
  • 法衣 - Refers to the monastic robes worn by Buddhist monks.
  • 讲经 - Refers to the act of delivering a sermon or lecture on Buddhist scriptures.
  • 比丘与比丘尼 - Refers to monks and nuns, respectively, in the Buddhist tradition.
  • 威仪 - Refers to the dignified and respectful behavior expected of monks and nuns.

Proper Nouns

  • 韩世忠 - General Han Shizhong, a historical figure known for his military prowess and political defiance.
  • 秦桧 - Qin Hui, a historical figure known for his political intrigues and persecution of loyalists.
  • 西湖 - West Lake, a famous scenic area in Hangzhou.
  • 翠微亭 - Cuiwei Pavilion, a famous landmark on Feilai Peak.
  • 飞来峰 - Feilai Peak, a famous landmark in Hangzhou.
  • 云林 - The name of Lingyin Temple, also known as 'Cloud Forest Temple.'
  • 帝辉 - The name of the abbot, Dihui, who is about ninety years old.

[622]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 愚民绝其父子之天性、饮食男女之大欲而为僧 - Ordinary people, by becoming monks, sever their natural familial ties and basic desires for food and sexual relations.
  • 盖有所不得已也 - There is a necessity or compulsion behind their actions.
  • 明先王之道以道之，鳏寡孤独废疾者有养也，则亦庶乎其可也 - If we clarify the ways of the ancient kings and provide for the destitute and disabled, then perhaps it can be managed.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 佛法 - Refers to Buddhism.
  • 中国 - Refers to China.
  • 愚民 - Refers to ordinary people or commoners.
  • 父子之天性 - Refers to the natural bond between father and son.
  • 饮食男女之大欲 - Refers to the basic desires for food and sexual relations.
  • 井田 - Refers to the well-field system, an ancient Chinese land distribution system.
  • 学校 - Refers to schools or educational institutions.
  • 先王之道 - Refers to the ways of the ancient kings, often associated with Confucian teachings.
  • 鳏寡孤独废疾者 - Refers to the destitute, widowers, widows, orphans, the elderly, and the disabled.
  • 欧阳子 - Refers to Ouyang Xiu, a prominent Chinese statesman, historian, and writer of the Song Dynasty.
  • 韩子 - Refers to Han Yu, a prominent Chinese writer and philosopher of the Tang Dynasty.

Proper Nouns

  • 欧阳子 - Ouyang Xiu.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 飛來峰 - Literally 'Flying Peak,' a famous mountain in Hangzhou, Zhejiang Province, known for its scenic beauty and cultural significance.
  • 下天竺 - Refers to the Lower Tianzhu Temple, one of the four major temples in the Tianzhu area.
  • 中天竺 - Refers to the Middle Tianzhu Temple.
  • 上天竺 - Refers to the Upper Tianzhu Temple.
  • 男女雜糅 - Means 'men and women mixed together,' indicating a chaotic or disordered scene.
  • 瓦砾场中烧香 - Literally 'burning incense in a field of tiles and rubble,' suggests a place of ruin or desolation.
  • 赤手挽银河 - A metaphorical expression meaning 'pulling the Milky Way with bare hands,' symbolizing heroic or extraordinary deeds.
  • 君自大名垂宇宙 - Means 'your great name has been inscribed in the universe,' praising someone's lasting fame.
  • 青山埋白骨 - Literally 'green hills bury white bones,' a phrase often used to describe the burial of heroes or the passing of time.
  • 我来何处哭英雄 - Means 'where do I come to weep for heroes,' expressing a sense of loss or mourning.
  • 烟霞岭 - Refers to a mountain ridge known for its misty and hazy scenery.
  • 极目皆山 - Means 'all one sees is mountains,' describing a landscape dominated by mountains.
  • 幽深奇伟 - Describes a place as deep, secluded, and magnificent.
  • 灵隐、天竺之间 - Refers to the area between Lingyin Temple and the Tianzhu Temples, known for its scenic beauty.
  • 吾始知吾之足力不能遍至也 - Means 'I began to realize that my strength could not reach all places,' indicating a sense of limitation.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 同年 - Refers to fellow candidates or peers who passed the imperial examinations in the same year.
  • 湖上之景未遍觀也 - Literally means 'the scenery of the lake has not been fully observed,' implying that the speaker has not yet seen all the attractions of the West Lake.
  • 循白堤 - Means 'to follow the Bai Causeway,' a famous scenic route in Hangzhou.
  • 孤山 - Refers to Solitary Hill, a prominent landmark in Hangzhou.
  • 行宮 - Means 'imperial palace,' but in this context, it likely refers to a former imperial garden or pavilion.
  • 復閣重廊 - Describes a complex of overlapping buildings and corridors.
  • 鑿石為基，削岩成壁 - Literally means 'carving stone to form the base, cutting rock to create walls,' describing the construction of the palace.
  • 引水成池，植花成幄 - Means 'drawing water to form a pond, planting flowers to create canopies,' describing the landscaping.
  • 橋水磴山 - Means 'bridging water, climbing mountains,' describing the layout of the palace.
  • 含岩石於殿中，注清泉於座下 - Means 'incorporating rocks within the hall, flowing clear springs beneath the seats,' describing the interior design.
  • 左右高樓，近挹湖光 - Means 'high buildings on both sides, close to the lake's light,' describing the view from the palace.
  • 遠吞山色 - Means 'far-reaching, swallowing the mountain colors,' describing the expansive view.
  • 如登玉霄金闕 - A metaphorical expression meaning 'like ascending to the jade heavens and golden palaces,' describing the grandeur of the palace.
  • 十洲三島之仙蹤 - Refers to the mythical Ten Isles and Three Islands, known for their beauty and the presence of immortals.
  • 放鶴亭 - Means 'Pavilion for Releasing Cranes,' a specific location within the palace.
  • 古梅巨石 - Means 'ancient plum trees and large stones,' describing the natural features around the pavilion.
  • 清雅不群 - Means 'clearly elegant and unique,' describing the aesthetic quality of the pavilion.
  • 惜亭殊巨麗 - Means 'regretting that the pavilion is unusually grand and beautiful,' suggesting a contrast with the expected simplicity of a hermit's retreat.
  • 下亭，復登舟 - Means 'descending from the pavilion, then boarding the boat again.'
  • 繞孤山之背 - Means 'circling the back of Solitary Hill.'
  • 昭慶寺 - Refers to Zhaqing Temple, a famous Buddhist temple in Hangzhou.
  • 於湖中之景，不能十一 - Means 'the scenery of the lake, not even a tenth of it,' indicating that the speaker has only seen a small portion of the lake's beauty.
  • 已暮矣 - Means 'it is already evening,' indicating the time of day.
  • 轎與船之不能遠到 - Means 'carriages and boats cannot reach far,' suggesting that these modes of transportation limit the scope of exploration.
  • 游西湖者未盡見西湖也 - Means 'those who visit West Lake do not see all of West Lake,' implying that a full appreciation of the lake requires more extensive exploration.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 留数日 - Literally 'stay for several days,' used to describe a brief period of time.
  • 遂渡江而东 - Means 'then cross the river to the east,' indicating a directional movement.
  • 亦有两山 - Means 'there are also two mountains,' used to describe the presence of additional geographical features.
  • 仿佛金、焦 - Means 'resembling Jin and Jiao,' referring to the similarity in appearance to the Jin and Jiao mountains.
  • 遥望海门，屹然对峙 - Means 'from afar, the sea gate stands opposite each other,' describing the distant view of the sea gate.
  • 惜时非八月，不能观大潮 - Means 'it is a pity that it is not August, so one cannot see the great tide,' expressing regret about the timing.
  • 渡江至萧山 - Means 'cross the river to Xiaoshan,' indicating a specific destination.
  • 萧山有湖，产蓴丝嘉鱼 - Means 'Xiaoshan has a lake that produces chun silk and fine fish,' describing the local resources.
  • 旱则引湖水以溉田，潦泄于海 - Means 'in drought, lake water is drawn to irrigate the fields; in flood, it is released into the sea,' describing the management of water resources.
  • 风景似西湖，而有用过之 - Means 'the scenery resembles West Lake, but is more useful,' comparing the beauty and utility of the lake.
  • 萧山东则山阴道上矣 - Means 'east of Xiaoshan is the Shanyin Road,' indicating a specific location.
  • 千岩万壑，大者奇伟，小者佳丽 - Means 'thousands of rocks and valleys, the large ones are strange and magnificent, the small ones are beautiful and charming,' describing the landscape.
  • 山下皆水，大溪小港，经纬绣错 - Means 'below the mountains are waters, large streams and small creeks, interwoven like embroidery,' describing the waterways.
  • 东至白鹤浦，有小山，舟人指曰：“禹戮防风氏之所也。” - Means 'east to Baigehu, there is a small mountain, and the boatman points and says, 'This is where Yu killed Fangfeng.' Describing a historical site.
  • 泛舟入山阴城，登卧龙山 - Means 'sailing into the city of Shanyin, climb Mount Wulong,' describing a journey.
  • 出城至于鉴湖 - Means 'leave the city and reach the Mirror Lake,' indicating a destination.
  • 昔明皇赐贺知章鉴湖一曲，后遂指此一曲为鉴湖 - Means 'in the past, Emperor Mingzong granted a section of Mirror Lake to He Zhizhang, and later this section was called Mirror Lake,' describing a historical event.
  • 其实萧山、会稽、山阴三县之水，皆鉴湖也 - Means 'in fact, the waters of Xiaoshan, Kuaiji, and Shanyin counties are all part of Mirror Lake,' clarifying the extent of the lake.
  • 尝登山而望之，三县桑田，其平如砥，想皆沧海所变 - Means 'once climbing the mountain and looking down, the three counties' fields are as flat as a grindstone, and it is thought they were all once part of the sea,' describing the landscape.
  • 水在其中，渟满不流，而色清若镜，故曰鉴湖也 - Means 'the water in it is still and does not flow, and its color is clear like a mirror, hence it is called Mirror Lake,' describing the characteristics of the lake.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 自鉴湖欲游吼山 - Wishing to travel from Jian Lake to Huoshan Mountain; '自' means 'from' and '欲' means 'to wish to'.
  • 鉴湖之水无波 - The water of Jian Lake is calm; '无波' means 'without waves' or 'calm'.
  • 梦中不知泊于何处 - In a dream, not knowing where we had moored; '泊' means 'to moor' or 'to anchor'.
  • 初无雨 - At first there was no rain; '初' means 'at first' or 'initially'.
  • 四围皆山 - Surrounded by mountains on all sides; '四围' means 'all around'.
  • 并无来路 - There was no way back; '并无' means 'not at all' or 'none at all'.
  • 乳泉乱滴如檐溜 - Milk-like springs dripping randomly like rainwater from eaves; '乱滴' means 'dripping randomly' and '檐溜' means 'rainwater dripping from eaves'.
  • 东峰有洞，水满其中 - The eastern peak has a cave, filled with water inside; '满' means 'filled' or 'full'.
  • 西峰怪石超出，长垂下注 - The western peak has peculiar stones protruding, hanging long and flowing downward; '超出' means 'protruding' and '下注' means 'flowing downward'.
  • 若巨象舒鼻以饮潭水 - As if a giant elephant was stretching its trunk to drink from the pond; '舒鼻' means 'stretching the trunk'.
  • 其北竹林茂密，楼阁清幽 - To the north, dense bamboo forests and serene pavilions; '茂密' means 'dense' and '清幽' means 'serene' or 'quietly beautiful'.
  • 晓梦初醒，疑非尘世 - Upon waking from a morning dream, one doubts it is not a worldly place; '晓梦' means 'morning dream' and '疑非尘世' means 'doubts it is not a worldly place'.
  • 舟人语曰 - The boatman said; '语曰' means 'said'.
  • 此所谓曹溪 - This is what is called Caoxi; '所谓' means 'what is called' or 'so-called'.
  • 东有洞者狮山，西如鼻者象山，有楼阁者，石匮先生之书院也 - The eastern one with a cave is Shishan (Lion Mountain), the western one resembling a nose is Xiangshan (Elephant Mountain), and the one with pavilions is the academy of Shikuai Xiansheng; '所谓' means 'what is called' or 'so-called'.
  • 登楼四望 - Climbing the tower to look around; '四望' means 'to look around'.
  • 见楼后之山尤高峻 - Seeing the mountain behind the tower especially high and steep; '尤' means 'especially' and '高峻' means 'high and steep'.
  • 怪石森列 - Strange stones arranged in a forest-like manner; '森列' means 'arranged in a forest-like manner'.
  • 有如台者，如柱者，如首戴笠者，如巨人立者 - Some are like platforms, some like columns, some like people wearing hats, and some like standing giants; '有如' means 'like' and '台' means 'platform', '柱' means 'column', '首戴笠' means 'wearing a hat', and '巨人立' means 'standing giants'.
  • 所谓吼山也 - This is what is called Huoshan (Roaring Mountain); '所谓' means 'what is called' or 'so-called'.
  • 下楼棹舟 - Descending the tower and rowing the boat; '棹舟' means 'rowing the boat'.
  • 由狮山之洞中，曲折行数百步而后出 - Through the cave of Shishan, winding through several hundred steps before emerging; '曲折' means 'winding' and '数百步' means 'several hundred steps'.
  • 如渔郎自桃源归也 - Like the fisherman returning from Peach Blossom Spring; '渔郎' means 'fisherman' and '桃源' means 'Peach Blossom Spring'.
  • 吼山有空明庵 - Huoshan has the Kōngmíng Temple; '空明庵' means 'Kōngmíng Temple'.
  • 门前流水，门内清池 - In front of the temple, flowing water; inside the temple, a clear pool; '门前' means 'in front of the temple' and '门内' means 'inside the temple'.
  • 朱楼碧瓦，倒影池中 - Red towers and green tiles, reflected in the pool; '朱楼' means 'red towers' and '碧瓦' means 'green tiles'.
  • 高岩峭壁，卓立楼后 - High cliffs and steep walls, standing tall behind the tower; '高岩' means 'high cliffs' and '峭壁' means 'steep walls'.
  • 瀑泉飞洒，常如骤雨 - Waterfalls flying and splashing, often like sudden rain; '瀑泉' means 'waterfalls' and '骤雨' means 'sudden rain'.
  • 其奇不减曹溪也 - Its wonders are not less than those of Caoxi; '不减' means 'not less than' and '曹溪' means 'Caoxi'.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 鉴湖 - Jian Lake, a famous lake in Zhejiang Province, known for its scenic beauty.
  • 吼山 - Huoshan Mountain, a mountain known for its natural beauty and historical significance.
  • 曹溪 - Caoxi, a place associated with the famous Zen master Huineng, known for its spiritual and cultural significance.
  • 狮山 - Shishan (Lion Mountain), a mountain with a cave, symbolizing strength and protection.
  • 象山 - Xiangshan (Elephant Mountain), a mountain resembling an elephant, symbolizing wisdom and longevity.
  • 石匮先生之书院 - The academy of Shikuai Xiansheng, a historical educational institution.
  • 空明庵 - Kōngmíng Temple, a temple known for its serene and peaceful atmosphere.
  • 渔郎自桃源归 - A reference to the story of the fisherman returning from Peach Blossom Spring, a metaphor for a utopian or idyllic place.

[627]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 吼山返棹 - Poetic expression meaning 'returning the oar after rowing through the howling mountains,' suggesting a journey through rugged terrain.
  • 乃谒禹陵 - '乃' is a conjunction meaning 'then' or 'thereupon,' and '谒' means 'to visit' or 'to pay respects.' Together, they indicate the action of visiting the Yu Mausoleum.
  • 高圆若冢 - Descriptive phrase meaning 'high and round like a grave mound,' used to describe the shape of the mountain.
  • 众峰环拱 - Means 'surrounded by peaks,' indicating that the mountain is encircled by other mountains, which serve as a protective or respectful barrier.
  • 有如侍卫 - Means 'as if serving as guards,' further emphasizing the protective nature of the surrounding peaks.
  • 陵侧有菲泉 - '陵侧' means 'beside the mausoleum,' and '菲泉' refers to a spring named 'Fei Spring,' which is known for its clear and pure water.
  • 泉东有庙 - '泉东' means 'east of the spring,' and '庙' refers to a temple dedicated to the worship of Yu.
  • 窆石亭 - '窆石' refers to a stone used in ancient burial practices, and '亭' means 'pavilion' or 'small building.' The term '窆石亭' thus refers to a pavilion housing the stone.
  • 相传葬禹时所用 - '相传' means 'it is said' or 'according to tradition,' and '葬禹时所用' means 'used during Yu's burial.' This phrase indicates that the stone was traditionally believed to have been used in Yu's burial.
  • 石高五六尺 - Means 'the stone is five or six feet high,' providing a specific measurement.
  • 烟如柱 - Means 'smoke-like' or 'pillar-like,' describing the shape of the stone.
  • 端有圆孔 - Means 'with a round hole at the top,' indicating a distinctive feature of the stone.
  • 似孔庙之汉碑 - '似' means 'like' or 'resembling,' and '孔庙之汉碑' refers to a Han dynasty stele found in the Confucian temple. This comparison suggests that the stone has a similar appearance to these ancient inscribed stones.
  • 公室视丰碑，三家视桓楹 - '公室' refers to the official residence, '丰碑' means 'tall stone monument,' '三家' refers to the three major families, and '桓楹' means 'tall pillars.' This phrase is from the *Book of Rites* and describes the different standards of mourning for different social classes.
  • 窆石似楹，盖葬碑也 - '窆石似楹' means 'the burial stone resembles a pillar,' and '盖葬碑也' means 'it is likely a burial stone.' This phrase suggests that the stone is a type of burial monument.
  • 南镇者，会稽山也 - '南镇者' means 'the Southern Mountain,' and '会稽山' is the name of the mountain. This phrase identifies the Southern Mountain as Mount Kuaiji.
  • 其最高者曰炉峰 - '其最高者' means 'the highest peak,' and '曰炉峰' means 'is called Furnace Peak.' This phrase identifies the highest peak of Mount Kuaiji.
  • 其下有庙，为历代祭告之所 - '其下' means 'below,' '庙' means 'temple,' and '为历代祭告之所' means 'where sacrifices and prayers have been offered throughout the ages.' This phrase describes the temple located below Furnace Peak.
  • 自南镇回舟 - '自' means 'from,' '南镇' is the Southern Mountain, and '回舟' means 'returning the boat.' This phrase indicates the return journey from the Southern Mountain.
  • 夜泊山阴城外 - '夜泊' means 'anchored at night,' and '山阴城外' means 'outside the city of Shanyin.' This phrase describes the location where the boat was anchored.
  • 月几望矣 - Means 'the moon is almost full,' indicating the phase of the moon.
  • 气霁云敛 - '气霁' means 'clearing weather,' and '云敛' means 'clouds gathering.' This phrase describes the weather conditions.
  • 月白江清 - '月白' means 'bright moon,' and '江清' means 'clear river.' This phrase describes the clear and bright night scene.
  • 天水相涵 - Means 'the sky and water blend together,' describing the harmonious and serene atmosphere.
  • 空明一片 - Means 'a vast expanse of clarity,' emphasizing the clear and bright night.
  • 人在舟中，身心朗彻 - '人在舟中' means 'people in the boat,' and '身心朗彻' means 'mind and body clear and bright.' This phrase describes the mental and physical state of the people.
  • 如琉璃合 - Means 'like a piece of clear crystal,' emphasizing the clarity and purity.
  • 恍然若有所悟 - Means 'suddenly as if one has gained insight,' describing a moment of enlightenment or realization.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 禹陵 - The mausoleum of Yu the Great, a legendary figure in Chinese history known for his efforts in flood control and the establishment of the Xia Dynasty.
  • 菲泉 - A spring located near the Yu Mausoleum, known for its clear and pure water.
  • 窆石亭 - A pavilion housing a stone believed to have been used in Yu's burial, symbolizing the reverence and respect for Yu.
  • 炉峰 - The highest peak of Mount Kuaiji, which is part of the Southern Mountain.
  • 会稽山 - The name of the mountain, which is a significant landmark in the region.
  • 山阴城 - An ancient city located in present-day Shaoxing, Zhejiang Province.
  • 月几望矣 - Refers to the phase of the moon when it is almost full, a common poetic reference in Chinese literature.
  • 气霁云敛 - Describes a clear and calm weather condition, often associated with a sense of peace and tranquility.
  • 月白江清 - A common poetic description of a beautiful and serene night scene, often used to evoke a sense of harmony and beauty.

Proper Nouns

  • 禹陵 - The mausoleum of Yu the Great.
  • 菲泉 - A spring located near the Yu Mausoleum.
  • 窆石亭 - A pavilion housing a stone believed to have been used in Yu's burial.
  • 炉峰 - The highest peak of Mount Kuaiji.
  • 会稽山 - The name of the mountain.
  • 山阴城 - An ancient city located in present-day Shaoxing, Zhejiang Province.
  • 孔庙之汉碑 - A Han dynasty stele found in the Confucian temple.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 黎明至於蘭亭 - Literally means 'at dawn arrived at the Orchid Pavilion.' Sets the scene for the visit.
  • 非昔之蘭亭矣 - Means 'is no longer the Orchid Pavilion of old,' indicating a change over time.
  • 中立大碑，御書右軍序於其上 - Refers to a large stele placed in the center with an imperial inscription of Wang Xizhi's sequence on it.
  • 亭前為石成渠，以為曲水 - Describes the construction of a stone channel before the pavilion, designed as a winding waterway.
  • 崎岖跼蹐 - Describes the winding and cramped nature of the path.
  • 初無遠致 - Means 'lacks distant charm' or 'does not have a far-reaching appeal.'
  • 且不可以流觞 - Indicates that it is not suitable for the traditional activity of floating cups along the water.
  • 左右各凿一池，以為是“鹅池”與“墨池”也 - Refers to pools dug on either side, named 'Goose Pond' and 'Ink Pond.'
  • 亭西里许，曰天章寺 - Indicates a temple about a mile west of the pavilion, named Tianzhang Temple.
  • 然此皆人为之者，故有废兴 - States that all these places are man-made and thus subject to rise and fall.
  • 若所谓“崇山峻岭”、“清流激湍” - Refers to the famous descriptions of 'tall mountains and steep hills,' 'clear streams and swift currents.'
  • 依然在 - Means 'still exist' or 'remain unchanged.'
  • 蓋山陰之水不流，唯蘭渚湍急，潺潺於茂林修竹之間，風致又別也 - Explains that the water of Shan Yin does not flow, but only the rapid current of Lan Zhu flows gently through the dense forest and tall bamboo, giving a unique charm.
  • 返城中，登蕺山 - Describes returning to the city and climbing Mount Jixi.
  • 下有寺，乃右軍之舊第 - Refers to a temple below, which was the former residence of Wang Xizhi.
  • 其南有題扇橋 - Indicates a bridge to the south of the temple, named 'Fan Writing Bridge.'
  • 山下有書院，劉念台講學於此 - Describes a schoolhouse at the foot of the mountain where Liu Niantai taught.

  • 蕺山 - Mount Jixi, a mountain near the Orchid Pavilion.

  • 不辨西东 - Means 'cannot distinguish east from west,' indicating disorientation.
  • 瞭然 - Means 'clearly' or 'distinctly,' indicating a sudden clarity.
  • 盖绍兴之西南皆山，而东北近海 - A descriptive statement about the geographical layout of Shaoxing.
  • 吼山在东，兰亭在西，禹凌、南镇在其南，北有梅山，下有梅市，梅福之所居也 - A list of geographical locations and their significance.
  • 远望南镇之西，有高于南镇者，曰秦望，始皇帝刻石于此 - Describes a distant view and a historical site.
  • 又禹穴非禹陵也，禹藏书于宛委之山，曰禹穴 - Clarifies a misunderstanding about the location of Yu's tomb and his hidden books.
  • 又会稽有阳明洞，道书云第十一洞天 - Mentions another significant location and its religious significance.
  • 游人惮于登陟，舟所可至者至之，若高远幽深、神圣仙灵之遗迹，则惧而不果去 - Describes the behavior of tourists and their reluctance to explore certain areas.
  • 抑吾在绍兴凡三望海 - Indicates multiple occasions of looking at the sea from different vantage points.
  • 第见茫茫沙草而已，实未尝见水 - Describes the view of the sea as just sand and grass, without actually seeing water.
  • 吾犹怅然以山海之奇未尽探也 - Expresses a sense of disappointment and a desire to explore more.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 由绍兴复返杭州 - Literal: 'from Shaoxing returned to Hangzhou,' describes movement.
  • 登凤凰山，一名紫阳山 - 'Climbing Phoenix Mountain, also known as Ziyang Mountain,' where '一名' indicates an alternative name.
  • 昔高宗南渡，广杭城，包此山于苑内 - 'In the past, Emperor Gaozong crossed south, expanded Hangzhou city, and included this mountain within the park,' where '昔' refers to a past event.
  • 以为游观之所 - 'As a place for leisure and sightseeing,' where '以为' means 'to regard as.'
  • 左江右湖 - 'With a river on the left and a lake on the right,' a descriptive phrase indicating geographical features.
  • 登临彷徨，致足乐也 - 'For climbing and wandering, it is truly delightful,' where '致足乐也' emphasizes the pleasure derived from the activity.
  • 自杭州溯浙江，至于富阳 - 'From Hangzhou, tracing upstream along the Zhejiang River, reaching Fuyang,' where '溯' means 'to trace upstream.'
  • 富阳之山，雄壮似燕秦诸塞，而青翠过之 - 'The mountains of Fuyang are majestic, resembling the passes of Yan and Qin, but greener,' where '而青翠过之' indicates a comparison.
  • 富阳以南，川势渐窄 - 'South of Fuyang, the river narrows,' where '川势' refers to the river's course.
  • 两山对峙，一水中流 - 'Two mountains face each other, with a river flowing between,' a vivid description of the landscape.
  • 群山倒影，上下皆青 - 'The reflection of the mountains, both above and below, is green,' where '上下皆青' emphasizes the green color.
  • 出橦梓关，势渐开 - 'Exiting the Tongzi Pass, the landscape gradually opens up,' where '势渐开' describes the changing scenery.
  • 远近布列，山皆妍媚 - 'Mountains are arranged far and near, all charming,' where '妍媚' means 'charming' or 'graceful.'
  • 桐君山陡立江岸 - 'Tongjun Mountain stands steeply by the riverbank,' where '陡立' emphasizes the steepness.
  • 其南内拓开一平原 - 'To the south, it opens up a plain,' where '内拓开' means 'to open up.'
  • 石壁环峙，如天生城阙 - 'Surrounded by cliffs, it looks like a naturally formed city gate,' where '如天生城阙' uses a simile.
  • 阻山临水，居民在山水之间 - 'Surrounded by mountains and water, residents live between the mountains and water,' where '阻山临水' describes the geographical setting.
  • 瓦青墙白，纤尘不染 - 'Roofs are blue, walls are white, without a speck of dust,' where '纤尘不染' emphasizes cleanliness.
  • 其清华朗润，令人神恬 - 'Its clarity and freshness are soothing to the spirit,' where '清华朗润' describes the environment.
  • 南至鸬鹚原，山势怪特 - 'South to the Lucheng Plain, the mountain range is strange and distinctive,' where '怪特' emphasizes uniqueness.
  • 峰峦坌涌，密峙骈植 - 'Peaks and ridges rise densely, standing closely together,' where '坌涌' and '密峙骈植' describe the mountainous terrain.
  • 束江流如一线 - 'Constraining the river to a single line,' where '束' means 'to constrain.'
  • 入原口转而西，则富春也 - 'Entering the plain's mouth and turning west, it is the Fuchun River,' where '则' indicates a conclusion.
  • 南北皆山，其中皆水，不余寸土 - 'North and south are mountains, in between is water, leaving no inch of land,' where '不余寸土' emphasizes the lack of land.

  • 众峰直起如筍 - Describes many peaks rising straight up like bamboo shoots, suggesting rapid growth and sharpness.
  • 有青山头，峰顶皆圆，有如人首 - Describes green mountains with round peaks resembling human heads.
  • 或冠或冕，或螓或颀，或光如僧，或鬟如妓 - Uses comparisons to describe the varied shapes of the mountain peaks, likening them to different types of people or hairstyles.
  • 寺隐丛篁，泉出古洞 - Describes a temple hidden among dense bamboo groves and a spring emerging from an ancient cave.
  • 棕榈芭蕉，延满岩谷 - Refers to palm and banana plants spreading throughout the rocky valleys.
  • 奇险幽秀，兼而有之 - Compound adjective describing the landscape as having all these qualities: strange, dangerous, secluded, and beautiful.
  • 西北至贵溪 - Indicates movement northwest to Guixi.

  • 又有象山，陆子静读书其上 - Refers to another mountain called Xiang Mountain, where Lu Zi Jing studied.

[635]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 湘江之水清而文 - Describes the clarity and beauty of the Xiang River. '清' means 'clear,' and '文' means 'beautiful' or 'ornate.'
  • 两岸之山秀而雅 - Describes the mountains along the river as graceful and elegant. '秀' means 'graceful,' and '雅' means 'elegant.'
  • 草多茅菅，扶疏猗靡 - Describes the vegetation as abundant and luxuriant. '扶疏' means 'luxuriant' or 'dense,' and '猗靡' means 'luxuriant' or 'luxuriantly growing.'
  • 蕙薄丛兰之致 - Refers to the fragrance and beauty of the grasses and reeds, similar to orchids and lilies. '蕙' and '兰' are both types of fragrant plants.
  • 五岭朝霞，三湘夜雨 - Describes the natural scenery of the Five Ridges and the Three Xiangs. '五岭' refers to the Five Ridges, a mountain range in southern China, and '三湘' refers to the Three Xiangs, a region in Hunan province.
  • 光风转蕙，皓月临枫 - Describes the gentle breeze turning the fragrance of the plants and the bright moon shining on the maples. '光风' means 'gentle breeze,' '转蕙' means 'turning the fragrance of the plants,' '皓月' means 'bright moon,' and '临枫' means 'shining on the maples.'
  • 吟《离骚》《九歌》《招魂》之句 - Refers to reciting lines from the works of Qu Yuan, a famous poet of the Warring States period. '离骚' is a long poem, '九歌' is a collection of songs, and '招魂' is a ritual text.
  • 如睹泽畔之憔悴也 - Means 'as if seeing the emaciated figure by the marsh.' '泽畔' refers to the marsh or lake shore, and '憔悴' means 'emaciated' or 'withered.'
  • 如逢芰衣荷裳之芳泽也 - Means 'as if encountering the fragrance of the lotus robes.' '芰衣' and '荷裳' both refer to garments made of lotus leaves.
  • 如闻湘灵山鬼之吟啸悲啼也 - Means 'as if hearing the singing and crying of the spirits of the Xiang River and the mountain ghosts.' '湘灵' refers to the spirits of the Xiang River, and '山鬼' refers to the mountain ghosts.
  • 南至衡州，谒南岳 - Means 'traveling south to Hengzhou to pay homage to Mount Heng.' '衡州' is the modern name for Hengyang, and '南岳' is the southern peak of the Five Great Mountains.
  • 非独形伟，其气盛也 - Means 'not only is its form magnificent, but its energy is also abundant.' '形伟' means 'magnificent form,' and '气盛' means 'abundant energy.'
  • 郁郁葱葱，灵光焕发 - Describes the lush and vibrant appearance of Mount Tai. '郁郁葱葱' means 'lush and vibrant,' and '灵光焕发' means 'radiant with spiritual light.'
  • 神采少减焉 - Means 'the divine aura is somewhat diminished.' '神采' means 'divine aura,' and '少减' means 'diminished.'
  • 兹见南岳，乃复如睹泰山 - Means 'seeing Mount Heng again, it is like seeing Mount Tai once more.' '兹' means 'now' or 'here,' and '复' means 'again.'
  • 连峰争出，高不可止 - Means 'the peaks rise one after another, reaching heights that cannot be surpassed.' '连峰' means 'connected peaks,' and '高不可止' means 'reaching heights that cannot be surpassed.'
  • 复岭互藏，厚不可穷 - Means 'the ridges interlock, forming depths that cannot be fathomed.' '复岭' means 'interlocking ridges,' and '厚不可穷' means 'forming depths that cannot be fathomed.'
  • 石壁插青，流泉界白 - Describes the green cliffs and white streams. '石壁插青' means 'green cliffs,' and '流泉界白' means 'white streams.'
  • 气勃如蒸，岚深似黛 - Describes the misty atmosphere. '气勃如蒸' means 'the air is thick and steamy,' and '岚深似黛' means 'the mist is deep and dark.'
  • 顶在云中，有若神龙 - Means 'the summit is in the clouds, like a divine dragon.' '顶在云中' means 'the summit is in the clouds,' and '有若神龙' means 'like a divine dragon.'
  • 火维地荒，天假神柄 - Means 'in the fiery realm of the wilderness, heaven grants divine power.' '火维' refers to the fiery realm, and '天假神柄' means 'heaven grants divine power.'
  • 衡山七十二峰，其最大者五 - Means 'Mount Heng has 72 peaks, with five being the largest.' '七十二峰' means '72 peaks,' and '其最大者五' means 'the five largest.'
  • 芙蓉、紫盖、石廪、天柱、祝融 - Names the five largest peaks of Mount Heng. '芙蓉' means 'cotton,' '紫盖' means 'purple canopy,' '石廪' means 'stone granary,' '天柱' means 'pillar of heaven,' and '祝融' means 'Zhurong.'
  • 南岳庙在祝融峰下，谒庙后，望五峰，其顶皆在云中 - Means 'the temple of Mount Heng is at the foot of Zhurong Peak. After paying respects at the temple, looking at the five peaks, their summits are all in the clouds.' '南岳庙' means 'temple of Mount Heng,' and '谒庙' means 'paying respects at the temple.'
  • 登舟南行数日，无时不矫首 - Means 'traveling south by boat for several days, one cannot help but raise one's head.' '登舟南行' means 'traveling south by boat,' and '无时不矫首' means 'one cannot help but raise one's head.'
  • 帆随湘转，望衡九面 - Means 'the sails follow the Xiang River, and one can see Mount Heng from nine directions.' '帆随湘转' means 'the sails follow the Xiang River,' and '望衡九面' means 'see Mount Heng from nine directions.'
  • 予九面望而卒未尝见其顶，始叹衡山之云之难开也 - Means 'I looked from nine directions but never saw the summit, and began to marvel at how difficult it is to see through the clouds of Mount Heng.' '予九面望' means 'I looked from nine directions,' '卒未尝见其顶' means 'never saw the summit,' and '始叹衡山之云之难开也' means 'began to marvel at how difficult it is to see through the clouds of Mount Heng.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 湘江 - The Xiang River, a major river in Hunan province, China.
  • 五岭 - The Five Ridges, a mountain range in southern China.
  • 三湘 - The Three Xiangs, a region in Hunan province.
  • 湘灵 - The spirits of the Xiang River.
  • 山鬼 - The mountain ghosts.
  • 衡州 - The modern name for Hengyang, a city in Hunan province.
  • 南岳 - The southern peak of the Five Great Mountains, located in Hengyang.
  • 泰山 - Mount Tai, one of the Five Great Mountains in China.
  • 衡山七十二峰 - Mount Heng has 72 peaks, with five being the largest.
  • 芙蓉、紫盖、石廪、天柱、祝融 - The five largest peaks of Mount Heng.
  • 南岳庙 - The temple of Mount Heng, located at the foot of Zhurong Peak.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • 湘江之水清而文，两岸之山秀而雅 - This phrase describes the beauty of the Xiang River and its surroundings. It can be read as two parallel clauses: 'the water of the Xiang River is clear and beautiful' and 'the mountains along the river are graceful and elegant.'
  • 草多茅菅，扶疏猗靡，皆有蕙薄丛兰之致 - This phrase describes the lush and fragrant vegetation. It can be read as a series of descriptive clauses: 'the grass is abundant and luxuriant, with the fragrance of orchids and lilies.'
  • 五岭朝霞，三湘夜雨 - This phrase describes the natural scenery of the Five Ridges and the Three Xiangs. It can be read as a pair of contrasting images: 'the morning glow over the Five Ridges' and 'the evening rain over the Three Xiangs.'

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 石阶直下数百级 - Literal: 'The stone steps descend straight down hundreds of levels.'
  • 顶上水纹如波 - Literal: 'The water ripples on the top like waves.'
  • 中有鲤鱼，长丈余，头目鳞尾皆具 - Literal: 'In the middle there is a carp, more than a zhang long, with head, eyes, scales, and tail all present.'
  • 洞后深黑，秉炬进数百步 - Literal: 'Behind the cave is deep and dark, holding a torch and advancing hundreds of steps.'
  • 冷气迫人 - Literal: 'The cold air presses on one.'
  • 同行者惧，遂偕出 - Literal: 'Those traveling together were afraid, so they all came out together.'
  • 闻土人道其中之景甚怪 - Literal: 'Hearing the locals say that the scenery inside is very strange.'
  • 王荆公云 - Literal: 'Wang Jingong said.'
  • 世上奇伟瑰怪非常之观 - Literal: 'In the world, there are extraordinary and strange sights.'
  • 常在于险远，而为人所罕至 - Literal: 'They often exist in dangerous and remote places, rarely visited by people.'
  • 故非有志者不能至也 - Literal: 'Therefore, only those with determination can reach them.'
  • 有志矣不随以止也 - Literal: 'Even if one has determination, it does not follow that one will stop.'
  • 然力不足者，亦不能至也 - Literal: 'However, those lacking strength cannot reach them either.'
  • 有志与力而又不随以怠 - Literal: 'Those with both determination and strength, yet do not follow with laziness.'
  • 至于幽暗昏忽，而无物以相之，亦不能至也 - Literal: 'When it comes to dark and dim places, without something to guide one, one cannot reach them either.'
  • 然力足以至焉，于人为可讥，而在己为有悔 - Literal: 'However, if one has the strength to reach them, it is something for others to criticize, and for oneself to regret.'
  • 尽吾志而不能至者，可以无悔矣 - Literal: 'If one has done their best and still cannot reach them, there is no regret.'
  • 吾甚悔吾之未尽吾志而随人以止也 - Literal: 'I deeply regret not having done my best and stopping with others.'
  • 其东有龙隐洞，清流从洞中出而入江 - Literal: 'To the east, there is a Dragon-Hiding Cave, where clear water flows out of the cave and into the river.'
  • 江中有山，轮囷若象鼻舒江中 - Literal: 'In the river, there is a mountain, shaped like an elephant's trunk, stretching out into the river.'
  • 舟行鼻内 - Literal: 'Boats travel inside the trunk.'
  • 江岸山上有洞，直透山背，以通天光 - Literal: 'On the mountain by the riverbank, there is a cave that goes straight through the back of the mountain, allowing sunlight to pass through.'
  • 望之圆明如满月 - Literal: 'Looking at it, it appears round and bright like a full moon.'
  • 志称“滨江三洞，水月最佳”者是也 - Literal: 'The records call it the best among the three caves by the river, known for its water and moonlight.'

  • 使志完居京师，得寒疾不汗，五日死矣 - A hypothetical statement suggesting that if Zhizheng had stayed in the capital, he would have died from a cold without sweating within five days.

  • 皆已湮没 - All have perished or been forgotten, indicating a loss of memory or recognition.
  • 谪特传 - Special transmission or legacy through exile, suggesting that Zhizheng's exile has given him a unique place in history.
  • 道乡台下有《岳麓寺碑》，李北海所书也 - The platform at Daoxiang Temple has a stele inscribed by Li Beihai, a famous calligrapher of the Tang Dynasty.
  • 凡地之美恶，视乎其人 - The beauty or ugliness of a place depends on the people who inhabit it, emphasizing the subjective nature of perception.
  • 不择地而安之，皆可安也 - One can find peace and contentment anywhere, regardless of the location.
  • 皆古迁客骚人痛哭流涕之所 - All these places are sites where exiled officials and poets have wept bitterly, highlighting the historical and emotional significance of these locations.
  • 入而游焉 - To enter and explore these places, suggesting a personal experience.
  • 瘴花善红 - Tropical flowers are red, indicating the exotic and vibrant nature of the environment.
  • 蛮鸟能语 - Local birds can speak, a poetic way of describing the unique sounds and characteristics of the region.
  • 水清石怪 - Clear water and strange stones, emphasizing the natural beauty and uniqueness of the landscape.
  • 皆有会心 - All have a deep connection or understanding, suggesting a personal and meaningful experience.
  • 已如更始余民，复睹司隶雍容 - It feels like being among the remnants of a new beginning, seeing the dignity and grace of the officials, indicating a sense of renewal and respect.
  • 少不更事 - Lack of experience or maturity, often used to describe someone who is too young or inexperienced to handle certain situations.
  • 北过橘州 - Passing through Juzhou in the north, indicating a specific journey.
  • 昔范质夫南谪，夫人每骂章惇 - In the past, when Fan Zhifu was exiled to the south, his wife often cursed Zhang Dun, highlighting the personal and political tensions of the time.
  • 公自负夫人以出，徐曰 - He carried his wife out of the boat, calmly saying, indicating a moment of calm and control in a difficult situation.
  • 此亦章惇为之耶 - Is this also the work of Zhang Dun? A rhetorical question suggesting that the situation is beyond their control.
  • 予性褊，服膺范公以自广 - I am narrow-minded, but I admire Fan Gong to broaden my horizons, indicating a personal reflection on one's character and influences.
  • 今过其地，想见其为人 - Now passing through this place, I can imagine his character, suggesting a personal and historical connection.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 北至于湘阴 - Literally 'to the north up to Xiangyin,' indicating a geographical limit.
  • 浩倍荡荡 - Describes the vastness and openness of the lake.
  • 四无涯涘 - Means 'boundless in all directions.'
  • 吾游行天下，山吾皆以为卑，水吾皆以为狭 - Expresses the author's subjective experience of traveling widely.
  • 目能穷其所至，则小之矣 - If one can see the limits, then it seems small.
  • 物何大何小，因其所大而大之，则莫不大；因其所小而小之，则莫不小 - Discusses the relativity of size based on perspective.
  • 苏子瞻曰 - Refers to Su Shi (Su Dongpo), a famous Song Dynasty poet and statesman.
  • 覆杯水于地，芥浮于水，蚁附于芥，茫然不知其所济 - A metaphor comparing a small amount of water to the vastness of the sea.
  • 少焉水涸，蚁即径去，见其类，出涕曰：几不复与子相见！ - Describes ants leaving when the water dries up, expressing a sense of loss.
  • 岂知俯仰之间，有方轨八达之路乎 - Questions the vastness of the world in a short span of time.
  • 计四海之在天地之间也，犹杯水也；舟犹芥也，人犹蚁也 - Compares the vastness of the four seas to a cup of water, boats to floating grass, and people to ants.
  • 吾乌知蚁之附芥，不以为是乘桴浮海耶？ - Questions whether ants think they are floating on the sea.
  • 其水涸而去，不以为是海变桑田耶？ - Questions whether ants think the sea has turned into land.
  • 四海虽广，应亦有涯，目力不至，则望洋而叹 - States that the four seas are vast but have limits, and if one cannot see them, they will sigh at their vastness.
  • 因所大而大之耳 - Emphasizes that things are perceived as large due to their actual size.
  • 今在洞庭，吾目力穷焉，即以为洞庭为吾之海可也 - Concludes that the Dongting Lake is vast enough to be considered the author's own sea.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 荆襄汉沔 - Refers to the regions of Jing, Xiang, Han, and Mian, which were historically significant areas in central China.

  • 方城为城，汉水为池，无所用众 - Fangcheng is like a city, and the Han River is like a moat, making it unnecessary to use a large army; emphasizes the natural defenses of the region.
  • 能伐楚者莫如秦 - No one can attack Chu better than Qin; highlights Qin's military prowess.
  • 出武关，下汉川，则撤荆襄之藩篱 - By exiting the Wusheng Pass and descending into the Han River valley, one can dismantle the defenses of Jing and Xiang; describes a strategic military move.
  • 出三峡，下夷陵，则扼鄂岳之要害 - By exiting the Three Gorges and descending into Yiling, one can control the vital points of E and Yue; describes another strategic military move.
  • 故秦并六国，亦地势然也 - Therefore, Qin's conquest of the six states was also due to the geographical advantages; concludes that Qin's success was partly due to its strategic location.

  • 日南影短，日北影长，日至之影，尺有五寸，即此也 - The shadow is shorter in the south and longer in the north. The shadow on the day of the solstice is fifteen inches long, which is this platform.

  • 达摩遗迹 - Refers to the traces or remains of Bodhidharma, the founder of Chan Buddhism.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 總而計之 - A formal expression meaning 'in summary' or 'when all is considered.'
  • 天下大勢 - Refers to the general trends or patterns of the world.
  • 水歸二漕 - Literally 'water returns to two canals,' likely referring to the two major river systems in China (the Yellow River and the Yangtze River).
  • 山分三干 - Literally 'mountains divide into three trunks,' referring to the three major mountain ranges in China (the northern, southern, and central ranges).
  • 河出昆仑 - Literally 'the river originates from Kunlun,' referring to the source of the Yellow River in the Kunlun Mountains.
  • 江源岷蜀 - Literally 'the river's source is in Min and Shu,' referring to the source of the Yangtze River in the Min and Sichuan regions.
  • 始于西极，入于东溟 - Literally 'beginning at the western extremity and ending in the eastern sea,' describing the flow of rivers from west to east.
  • 大河以北，水皆南流 - Literally 'north of the Great River, water all flows south,' describing the direction of rivers north of the Yellow River.
  • 大江以南，水皆北注 - Literally 'south of the Great River, water all flows north,' describing the direction of rivers south of the Yangtze River.
  • 汉南入江，淮北入河 - Literally 'the Han River flows into the Yangtze, the Huai River flows into the Yellow River,' describing the flow of these two major rivers.
  • 虽名“四渎”，犹之二也 - Literally 'although named 'Four Rivers,' they are still two,' referring to the four major rivers (Yellow, Yangtze, Huai, and Han) but emphasizing their dual nature.
  • 太行九边，西接玉门，东抵朝鲜 - Literally 'the Taihang Mountains extend to the nine borders, west to Yumen, east to Korea,' describing the geographical extent of the Taihang Mountains.
  • 五岭、衡、巫，西接峨嵋，东抵会稽 - Literally 'the Five Ranges, Heng, and Wuxia, west to Emei, east to Kuaiji,' describing the geographical extent of these mountain ranges.
  • 岷、嶓、华、嵩 - Refers to the Min, Ba, Huai, and Song mountains, which are part of the central mountain range.
  • 岱宗特起，不与嵩连 - Literally 'Mount Tai rises uniquely, not connected to Mount Song,' describing the geographical isolation of Mount Tai.
  • 北方水位，故燕、秦、三晋之山，色黑而陂陀若波 - Literally 'the northern water position, thus the mountains of Yan, Qin, and the three Jins are black and undulating like waves,' describing the characteristics of northern mountains.
  • 东方木也，故齐、鲁、吴、越之山，色青而森秀若林 - Literally 'the eastern wood, thus the mountains of Qi, Lu, Wu, and Yue are green and lush like forests,' describing the characteristics of eastern mountains.
  • 楚南、闽粤，峰尖而土赤 - Literally 'south of Chu, Fujian and Guangdong, peaks sharp and soil red,' describing the characteristics of southern mountains.
  • 粤西、黔、蜀，石白而形方 - Literally 'west of Guangdong, Guizhou, and Sichuan, stones white and shape square,' describing the characteristics of western mountains.
  • 天有五行，五方应之 - Literally 'heaven has five elements, the five directions correspond to them,' referring to the Chinese cosmological system of the five elements and directions.
  • 江性宽缓，河流湍急 - Literally 'the Yangtze has a broad and slow nature, the river has swift currents,' describing the characteristics of the Yangtze and other rivers.
  • 焦白鄱红，洞庭澄清 - Literally 'Jiaoluo is white, Poyang is red, Dongting is clear,' describing the characteristics of these lakes.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 斯行也 - Formal opening phrase meaning 'this journey' or 'this expedition.'
  • 四海滨其三 - Idiomatic expression meaning 'three of the four seas were seen.'
  • 九州历其七 - Idiomatic expression meaning 'seven of the nine regions were traversed.'
  • 五岳睹其四 - Idiomatic expression meaning 'four of the five sacred mountains were seen.'
  • 四渎见其全 - Idiomatic expression meaning 'all four major rivers were seen.'
  • 帝王之所都 - Idiomatic expression meaning 'where emperors and kings have established their capitals.'
  • 圣贤之所处 - Idiomatic expression meaning 'where sages and virtuous men have lived.'
  • 通都大邑 - Idiomatic expression meaning 'major cities and towns.'
  • 民物之所聚 - Idiomatic expression meaning 'where people and goods gather.'
  • 山川险塞 - Idiomatic expression meaning 'rugged mountains and rivers, natural barriers.'
  • 英雄之所争 - Idiomatic expression meaning 'places where heroes have contended.'
  • 古迹名胜 - Idiomatic expression meaning 'ancient sites and famous scenic spots.'
  • 文人学士之所歌咏 - Idiomatic expression meaning 'places that poets and scholars have praised.'
  • 魁奇磊落 - Idiomatic expression meaning 'outstanding and noble.'
  • 潜修独行 - Idiomatic expression meaning 'those who practice their virtues in seclusion and act independently.'
  • 伏处山颠水湄 - Idiomatic expression meaning 'hide in the high mountains and watersides.'
  • 溷迹渔樵负贩之中 - Idiomatic expression meaning 'blend in with fishermen, woodcutters, and peddlers.'
  • 岂造物者未之生耶？ - Rhetorical question meaning 'Could it be that such people have not been created by nature?'
  • 抑吾未之遇耶？ - Rhetorical question meaning 'Or could it be that I have not encountered them?'
  • 抑虽遇之而不识耶？ - Rhetorical question meaning 'Or could it be that I have met them but failed to recognize them?'
  • 吾憾焉！ - Expression meaning 'I feel regretful!'
  • 然苟吾心之善取 - Expression meaning 'However, if my heart is good at discernment.'
  • 则于山见仁者之静 - Expression meaning 'Then I can see the tranquility of the benevolent in the mountains.'
  • 于水见智者之动 - Expression meaning 'And the dynamism of the wise in the water.'
  • 其突兀汹涌，如睹勇士之叱咤 - Expression meaning 'The sudden and turbulent waves, like seeing a brave warrior's shout.'
  • 其沦涟娟秀，如睹淑人君子之温文也 - Expression meaning 'The gentle and graceful ripples, like seeing the gentle demeanor of a virtuous gentleman.'
  • 然则谓吾日遇其人焉，可也 - Conclusion meaning 'Therefore, it can be said that I encounter such people every day.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 抑又思之 - Rhetorical phrase meaning 'but further consider,' used to introduce a reflective or speculative thought.
  • 天地之化，阴阳而已 - Philosophical statement about the fundamental duality of the universe, where all transformation is driven by the interaction of yin and yang.
  • 独阴不生，独阳不成 - Traditional Chinese philosophical idea that yin and yang are complementary forces, and neither can exist independently.
  • 大漠之北不毛，而交、广以南多水 - Geographical observation that the northern deserts are barren, while the southern regions (Jiao and Guang) are lush and water-rich.
  • 文明发生，独此震旦之区而已 - Statement that civilization has arisen uniquely in the region of Zhendan (a term for China).
  • 北走胡而南走越，三月而可至 - Reference to the relative ease of travel between the northern and southern regions of China, which could be traversed in three months.
  • 昆仑至东海，半年之程耳 - Statement that the journey from Kunlun Mountain to the East Sea takes half a year, emphasizing the vastness of the land.
  • 大块亦甚小也 - Philosophical reflection that the world, despite its vastness, is relatively small when viewed from a certain perspective.
  • 吾以二月出都，河北之地，草芽未生 - Personal anecdote about the author leaving the capital in February, noting that the grass in the northern regions had not yet sprouted.
  • 至吴而花开，至越而花落 - Description of the seasonal changes observed during a journey, with flowers blooming in Wu and falling in Yue.
  • 入楚而栽秧，至粵而食稻 - Continuation of the journey, noting the agricultural activities in Chu and the consumption of rice in Guangdong.
  • 粤西返棹，秋老天高，至河南而木叶尽脱 - Description of the return journey, noting the autumn scenery and the shedding of leaves in Henan.

  • 时物过而生悲 - Statement that the passing of time and the changing of seasons can evoke sadness.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • 丐者行山中，见群儿缚一小猴而虐之 - This sentence describes a sequence of events where the beggar sees children tying up and maltreating a monkey.
  • 丐者买而教之戏，日乞于市，得钱甚多 - This sentence describes the beggar buying the monkey and teaching it to perform tricks, earning a lot of money.
  • 他丐忌且羡，因酒醉丐者，诱至空窑，椎杀于窑中 - This sentence describes other beggars envying and taking advantage of the beggar, luring him to an abandoned kiln and beating him to death.
  • 异日绳其猴，复使作戏 - This sentence describes the beggar's companions tying up the monkey again and making it perform tricks.
  • 而汪公呵导声遽至，猴即啮断绳，突走公之前，作冤诉状 - This sentence describes Mr. Wang arriving suddenly, shouting, and the monkey biting through the rope, running to him, and making a petition of grievance.
  • 公遣人随而往，得尸窑中 - This sentence describes Mr. Wang sending people to follow the monkey and finding the body in the kiln.
  • 亟捕他丐鞫问，伏法 - This sentence describes Mr. Wang immediately arresting the other beggars and interrogating them, leading to their confession and punishment.
  • 合邑骇而悼之，买棺焚丐者尸 - This sentence describes the entire county being shocked and mourning, and buying a coffin to burn the beggar's body.
  • 烈焰方发，猴哀叫跃入，死矣 - This sentence describes the fierce flames starting, the monkey crying out in sorrow and jumping into the fire, and dying.
  • 牛奔清前，跪而悲鸣 - This sentence describes a cow running to Squire Zhao, kneeling down, and mournfully neighing.
  • 谁氏之牛 - This sentence asks whose cow it is.
  • 臻其有冤乎 - This sentence asks if Wang Zhen has a grievance.
  • 抵邑，即辩释臻父子 - This sentence describes Squire Zhao arriving at the county and immediately acquitting Wang Zhen and his son.
  • 后鞫大盗王山，得其杀人状 - This sentence describes Squire Zhao later interrogating the notorious bandit Wang Shan and finding murder charges against him.

  • 私问曰 - 私问 means 'asked privately,' indicating a personal inquiry.

  • 志切云霄 - 志切 means 'strong desire,' and 云霄 refers to the heavens or high places. The phrase expresses a strong desire to reach high places.
  • 常恨羽翼未生 - 常恨 means 'often regret,' and 羽翼未生 means 'wings have not yet grown.' The phrase expresses a regret about lacking the means to achieve one's goals.
  • 九阍难叩 - 九阍 refers to the nine gates of heaven, and 难叩 means 'difficult to knock on.' The phrase indicates the difficulty of reaching the heavens.
  • 幸遘奇缘 - 幸遘 means 'fortunately encountered,' and 奇缘 means 'fate.' The phrase expresses a fortunate encounter with fate.
  • 汝浊世凡人也 - 浊世 means 'chaotic world,' and 凡人 means 'ordinary person.' The phrase describes the character as an ordinary person in a chaotic world.
  • 去则恐犯天令 - 去 means 'to go,' 则 is a conjunction meaning 'then,' and 恐犯天令 means 'fear violating heavenly decree.' The phrase expresses a concern about violating heavenly laws.
  • 汝可具所生年月日时来 - 具 means 'provide,' 所生年月日时 means 'date of birth,' and 来 means 'come.' The phrase asks for the date of birth.
  • 此人命有天禄 - 命 means 'fate,' 天禄 means 'heavenly reward.' The phrase indicates that the person's fate includes a heavenly reward.
  • 系忠直之裔 - 系 means 'is,' 忠直 means 'loyal and upright,' and 之裔 means 'descendant.' The phrase indicates that the person is a descendant of loyal and upright individuals.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • 居久之，忽有报者曰：‘闽船已到。’ This phrase introduces a sudden announcement about the arrival of a Fujian ship after a long time.
  • 王召见，赐白黑珠一囊，曰：‘以此偿造舟之价。’ This sequence describes the king summoning the person, granting them a bag of white and black pearls, and instructing them to use it to pay for the cost of building the boat.
  • 命小艇送附闽船，抵琼山还家。壬午之十二月也。 This phrase describes the king ordering a small boat to accompany the Fujian ship to Qiongshan and back home, specifying the date.
  • 家人蚤闻覆溺之信，设主发丧，乍见述祖，惊喜逾望。 This sequence describes the family hearing early about the shipwreck, holding a funeral, and suddenly seeing Shuzi, who was more surprised and delighted than expected.
  • 述祖亦不言所以，但云狂风败舟，幸凭‘擎天柱’遇救得免。 This phrase describes Shuzi not explaining the cause but saying that a strong wind capsized the boat, and they were rescued with the help of the 'Sky Support Pillar.'
  • 次年入广州，出囊中珠，鬻于番贾，获赀无算，买田终老。 This sequence describes Shuzi entering Guangzhou the following year, selling the pearls to foreign merchants, gaining an incalculable amount of wealth, and buying land to live out his days.
  • 康熙丙子，粤僧方趾麟亲访述祖，具得其详。时述祖年已九十六，貌如五十岁人。 This phrase describes a Buddhist monk named Fang Zilin personally visiting Shuzi in the year Bingzi of the Kangxi era, getting all the details, and noting that Shuzi was 96 years old but looked like a fifty-year-old person.
  • 张山来曰：若非有年月姓名，便如读《太平广记》矣。 This phrase introduces a commentator named Zhang Shanlai, who comments that without the date, year, and name, the story would be like reading the *Taiping Guangji*.
  • 先君尝疑李贺《白玉楼记》，谓九州万国语言文字，各不相同。今观此，则上天果与中华同矣。 This phrase introduces a commentator named Zhang Shanlai, who comments that without the date, year, and name, the story would be like reading the *Taiping Guangji*.

  • 雷所擊者 - Refers to the targets of lightning strikes.
  • 不孝與用銅為多 - Means 'filial impiety and the use of copper are more common.'
  • 光棍不與焉 - Means 'ruffians are not included among them.'
  • 何也 - A rhetorical question meaning 'why is that?'
  • 吾非謂不孝與用銅不當擊 - Means 'I do not mean that filial impiety and the use of copper should not be struck.'
  • 只以光棍為更當擊耳 - Means 'I only mean that ruffians deserve to be struck more.'
  • 雷之不及光棍 - Means 'lightning does not strike ruffians.'
  • 殆亦畏之耶 - Means 'is it perhaps afraid of them?'
  • 抑多而不勝擊邪 - Means 'or are there too many to strike?'
  • 高懷中，業鳝面於揚州小東門 - Means 'Gao Huazhong, who makes eel noodles at the small eastern gate of Yangzhou.'
  • 日殺鳝數千 - Means 'kills thousands of eels daily.'
  • 一婢憫之 - Means 'one servant girl pities him.'
  • 每夜分，竊缸中鳝 - Means 'every night, she steals the eels from the vat.'
  • 從後窗拋入河 - Means 'and throws them into the river from the back window.'
  • 如是積年 - Means 'like this for many years.'
  • 一日面店被焚 - Means 'one day, the noodle shop is burned down.'
  • 婢踉蹌逃出 - Means 'the servant girl stumbles out.'
  • 為火所傷，困臥河濱 - Means 'is injured by the fire and lies exhausted by the riverbank.'
  • 夜深睡去，比醒而痛減 - Means 'falls asleep at night, and when she wakes up, the pain has lessened.'
  • 火瘡盡愈 - Means 'the burns have all healed.'
  • 視之，有河中污泥，堆於瘡處 - Means 'looking at it, there is river mud piled on the wound.'
  • 而地有鳝行跡 - Means 'and there are traces of eels on the ground.'
  • 始知向者所放生來救之也 - Means 'she realizes that the eels she released before have come to save her.'
  • 按醫書：河底泥，能塗湯火傷 - Means 'according to medical texts, river mud can treat burns.'
  • 高感其異，遂為罷業 - Means 'Gao is moved by this and decides to stop his business.'
  • 及拆鍋，下有洞穴 - Means 'when he takes down the pot, there is a hole below.'
  • 生鳝數石盤其中，盡舉而縱之河 - Means 'there are several baskets of live eels inside, and he releases them all into the river.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 雷所擊者 - Refers to the traditional belief that lightning strikes those who are morally or socially undesirable.
  • 不孝 - Refers to the Confucian value of filial piety, where failing to honor one's parents is considered a grave sin.
  • 用銅 - Copper was often associated with wealth and luxury, which could be seen as a moral failing.
  • 光棍 - Refers to ruffians or thugs, often seen as morally corrupt.
  • 高懷中 - A historical figure known for his eel noodle shop.
  • 揚州小東門 - A specific location in Yangzhou, known for its eel noodle shops.
  • 醫書 - Refers to traditional Chinese medical texts, which often contained remedies using natural substances like river mud.

Proper Nouns

  • 張山來 - The name of the speaker.
  • 高懷中 - The name of the eel noodle shop owner.
  • 揚州小東門 - A specific location in Yangzhou.
  • 醫書 - Refers to traditional Chinese medical texts.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 此事若论功，当以潘为首，而朱次之 - If we consider merit, it should be ranked with Pan as the first and Zhu as the second. It implies a ranking based on merit or contribution.
  • 不报前之朱锦，而报于百余年后之同名者 - Not reporting the previous Zhu Jin, but reporting one who shares the same name after more than a hundred years. It suggests a discrepancy in historical records or a confusion between individuals with the same name.
  • 荒年 - Refers to a period of famine or scarcity.
  • 圣裔 - Means 'descendant of the sage' or 'sage's lineage,' typically referring to descendants of Confucius.
  • 圣胄散落 - Means 'the sage's descendants are scattered,' indicating a state of dispersion among the lineage.
  • 圣位 - Refers to a shrine or altar dedicated to Confucius or his descendants.
  • 无行 - Means 'having no virtue' or 'being immoral.'
  • 淫人之妻 - Means 'committing adultery with someone else's wife.'
  • 夫死，遂娶为妾 - Means 'after the husband died, she married him as a concubine.'
  • 己妻亦有淫行 - Means 'her own wife also had adulterous behavior.'
  • 乡里薄之 - Means 'the village people looked down on her.'
  • 包角巾罩于髻上 - Describes a type of headgear, specifically a headscarf wrapped around the bun on top of the head.
  • 方项有带者 - Refers to a band worn around the neck or shoulders.
  • 觉而骇甚 - Means 'awakening with great fear.'
  • 避之 - Means 'to avoid it.'
  • 火发 - Means 'fire broke out.'
  • 遂焚死 - Means 'and were burned to death.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 张山来曰 - 'Zhang Shanlai said,' indicating a speaker or narrator.
  • 潘为首，而朱次之 - Refers to a ranking system where Pan is considered superior to Zhu, possibly in terms of merit or contribution.
  • 百余年后之同名者 - Refers to someone sharing the same name as Zhu Jin, but living more than a hundred years later, suggesting a historical or genealogical error.
  • 仪真孔姓者 - 'A man named Kong from Yizhen,' referring to a specific individual.
  • 圣裔 - Refers to the lineage of Confucius, a significant cultural and historical figure.
  • 圣胄散落 - Indicates a state of dispersion among the Confucian lineage, possibly due to historical events.
  • 圣位 - Refers to a shrine dedicated to Confucius, emphasizing the importance of Confucianism in Chinese culture.
  • 无行 - Refers to a lack of moral virtue, which was highly stigmatized in traditional Chinese society.
  • 淫人之妻 - Adultery was a serious offense in traditional Chinese society, often leading to social ostracism.
  • 夫死，遂娶为妾 - Remarriage after the death of a spouse was common, but marrying a deceased husband's concubine was unusual and potentially scandalous.
  • 己妻亦有淫行 - Again, adultery was a serious offense, and the fact that both the woman and her husband's concubine were involved adds to the scandal.
  • 乡里薄之 - The village people's disapproval reflects the strong social norms and expectations in traditional Chinese society.
  • 塾师 - Refers to a private school teacher, indicating the educational system of the time.
  • 夜梦 - Dreaming was often interpreted as having spiritual or prophetic significance in traditional Chinese culture.
  • 司马牛 - A historical figure, possibly a reference to a disciple of Confucius.
  • 包角巾罩于髻上 - A specific type of headgear, reflecting the fashion and customs of the time.
  • 方项有带者 - Another detail about the headgear, adding to the description of the dream.
  • 觉而骇甚 - The dreamer's fear indicates the perceived importance or danger of the dream.
  • 避去 - The action taken to avoid the predicted disaster.
  • 火发 - Fire was a significant natural disaster in traditional Chinese society, often associated with divine retribution.
  • 遂焚死 - The tragic outcome of the fire, emphasizing the severity of the punishment.

  • 张山来 - Zhang Shanlai, a person mentioned in the text.
  • 潘 - Pan, a person mentioned in the text.
  • 朱 - Zhu, a person mentioned in the text.
  • 朱锦 - Zhu Jin, a specific individual mentioned in the text.
  • 仪真 - Yizhen, a place name.
