Idiomatic Expressions

  • 此事予犹及见之 - Indicates that the speaker has personally witnessed or heard about the event.
  • 然亦此人不肖，故遭此报耳 - "然" introduces a contrast, and "不肖" means "unworthy" or "undeserving." "故" indicates causality, and "遭此报" means "suffered this retribution."
  • 年十七，忽一人扣户，称江北某家，延请角技 - "忽" means "suddenly," and "扣户" means "knocking on the door." "称" means "claiming," and "江北某家" refers to a family in northern Jiang. "延请" means "invite," and "角技" means "to compete in skills."
  • 君辅袱被随之往，月余，抵中州某宦宅 - "袱被" means "packing bedding," and "随之往" means "followed along." "月余" means "more than a month," and "抵中州某宦宅" means "arrived at the residence of a certain official in Zhongzhou."
  • 宦信之 - "宦信之" means "the official believed him."
  • 君辅方久坐堂上，讶无出肃客者 - "君辅" refers to Jiang Junfu, "方久坐堂上" means "was sitting in the hall for a long time." "讶" means "surprised," and "无出肃客者" means "no one came out to receive guests."
  • 江惊异，厉声争之 - "江" refers to Jiang Junfu, "惊异" means "surprised and puzzled." "厉声" means "shouting sternly," and "争之" means "arguing against it."
  • 出所著《奕谱》呈宦证之 - "出所著" means "produced his," "《奕谱》" means "the chess manual," "呈宦" means "presented to the official," and "证之" means "to prove it."
  • 甫对着，君辅连胜数局 - "甫" means "just," and "对着" means "started playing." "君辅" refers to Jiang Junfu, "连胜" means "won consecutively," and "数局" means "several games."
  • 宦爽然，深相礼貌 - "宦" refers to the official, "爽然" means "disappointed and surprised." "深相" means "deeply," and "礼貌" means "courteous."
  • 其地有国手，从无出其右 - "其地" means "in that place," "有" means "has," and "国手" means "national master." "从无" means "never," and "出其右" means "better than him."
  • 宦忽请对局，辅又连胜 - "宦" refers to the official, "忽" means "suddenly," and "请对局" means "asked to play a game." "辅" refers to Jiang Junfu, "又" means "again," and "连胜" means "won consecutively."
  • 宦大喜，待为上客 - "宦" refers to the official, "大喜" means "greatly pleased." "待" means "treated," "为" means "as," and "上客" means "guest of honor."
  • 盘桓数月，作书叠荐好奕钜公处 - "盘桓" means "lingered," and "数月" means "for several months." "作书" means "wrote letters," "叠荐" means "repeatedly recommended," "好奕" means "good at chess," and "钜公" means "high-ranking officials."
  • 获金数百归 - "获金" means "earned money," "数百" means "hundreds of," and "归" means "returned home."

  • 柳轩丛谈 - A collection of anecdotes or stories.
  • 寄园寄所寄 - Another collection of anecdotes or stories.
  • 婺源 - A county in Jiangxi Province, known for its cultural heritage.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 此當是某宦故作狡狯耳 - Phrase meaning 'This is likely a trick or prank by a certain official.' '狡狯' refers to cunning or mischievous behavior.
  • 不然，賣子為僕，豈不睹面而遽成交邪 - Phrase meaning 'Otherwise, selling one's son as a servant, wouldn't one have to meet in person before making such a deal?' '遽' means 'hastily' or 'immediately.'
  • 篆學圖書，多出於新安，為他郡所不及 - Phrase meaning 'Seal carving and calligraphy works are mostly from Xin'an, surpassing those of other counties.' '所不及' means 'cannot match.'
  • 真草隸篆，以及諸家書法，無所不精 - Phrase meaning 'He is proficient in all styles of calligraphy, including regular, cursive, clerical, and seal scripts, as well as various schools of calligraphy.'
  • 每寫一家，從不致雜入一笔 - Phrase meaning 'Whenever he writes in one style, he never mixes in a single stroke from another style.'
  • 大則一字方丈，小則徑寸千言 - Phrase meaning 'Large characters can be as big as a square zhang, while small ones can fit a thousand words in an inch.'
  • 鐵筆之妙，包羅百家，前無古人 - Phrase meaning 'The beauty of his brushwork encompasses all schools, unmatched by anyone before him.'
  • 少時至楚中販米 - Phrase meaning 'In his youth, he traveled to Chu to sell rice.'
  • 偶至一寺，見衣冠者十余輩 - Phrase meaning 'Once, he visited a temple and saw more than ten well-dressed men.'
  • 在佛殿以沙聚地，成字徑丈 - Phrase meaning 'They gathered sand on the ground in the Buddha hall, forming a character as large as a zhang.'
  • 守嘆賞久之 - Phrase meaning 'The governor admired it for a long time.'
  • 至今樓雖屢修，而此匾不能易也 - Phrase meaning 'Even though the building has been repaired many times, this plaque remains unchanged.'
  • 其徒王言，字纶紫，北門人 - Phrase meaning 'His disciple Wang Yan, courtesy name Lunzi, is from the northern gate.'
  • 篆書出宦光之上，隸書直追中郎 - Phrase meaning 'His seal script surpasses that of Guan Guang, and his clerical script rivals that of Zhong Rang.'
  • 至於行楷，各盡其妙 - Phrase meaning 'As for running and regular script, he excels in each.'

  • 然不知为紫云、为杨枝也 - 'But it's unclear whether it's Ziyun or Yangzhi.' This is a pun or wordplay, as '紫云' (Ziyun) and '杨枝' (Yangzhi) can also refer to a type of tree or plant, but here they are used to refer to a person.
  • 珍重之意，逾于掌珠 - 'The affection is more precious than a pearl on one's palm.' '掌珠' (zhǎng zhū) is a metaphor for a beloved daughter or object.
  • 饲以精粲嘉鱼，过餍而毙 - 'Fed with finely prepared fish, it died from overeating.' '精粲嘉鱼' (jīng càn jiā yú) means 'finely prepared fish,' and '过餍' (guò yàn) means 'overeating.'
  • 夫人惋悒累日，至为辍膳 - 'The lady was grieved for several days, even to the point of stopping her meals.' '惋悒' (wǎn yì) means 'grieved,' and '辍膳' (chuò shàn) means 'to stop eating.'
  • 宗伯特以沉香斫棺瘗之 - 'The Master specially made a coffin of sandalwood and buried it.' '斫棺' (zhuó guān) means 'to make a coffin,' and '瘗之' (yì zhī) means 'to bury it.'
  • 延十二女僧，建道场三昼夜 - 'He invited twelve female monks to hold a ritual for three days and nights.' '延' (yán) means 'to invite,' and '建道场' (jiàn dào chǎng) means 'to hold a ritual.'
  • 此猫享用太过，但不识工于捕鼠否？ - 'This cat was too pampered, but I wonder if it was good at catching mice?' '享用太过' (xiǎng yòng tài guò) means 'too pampered,' and '工于捕鼠' (gōng yú bǔ shǔ) means 'good at catching mice.'

  • 不耐卑庳下湿 - Means 'cannot tolerate low, damp places,' indicating a dislike for low-lying or humid environments.

  • 晨起披襟，爽受风日 - Literally 'in the morning, I open my robe and enjoy the cool breeze and sunlight,' describing the pleasure of waking up and feeling refreshed by the outdoors.
  • 如入闇室幽暧，便闷欲绝 - Means 'like entering a dark, damp room, it makes one feel suffocated and nearly faint,' contrasting the discomfort of a poorly ventilated space.
  • 舟行，放浆芦洲蓼渚之间 - Refers to 'sailing a boat, letting the oars drift in the reed marshes and lotus ponds,' describing a serene and natural setting.

  • 舟行 - Refers to traveling by boat, which was a common mode of transportation in ancient China, especially along rivers and lakes.
  • 芦洲蓼渚 - Refers to reed marshes and lotus ponds, which were typical natural landscapes in ancient China, often associated with tranquility and beauty.

  • 孙子数人 - 孙子 refers to Sunzi, a famous ancient Chinese military strategist, but here it seems to mean 'the young ones' or 'the children.' 数人 means 'counting people' or 'listing people.'
  • 与长者点定文字 - 与 means 'together with,' 长者 means 'elder' or 'wise person,' 点定 means 'to correct' or 'to revise,' and 文字 means 'writing' or 'text.'
  • 粗为疏解 - 粗 means 'roughly' or 'briefly,' 疏解 means 'to explain' or 'to interpret.'
  • 群小则牵绕衣裙 - 群小 means 'a group of young ones' or 'children,' 则 means 'then,' 牵绕 means 'to tangle' or 'to entangle,' and 衣裙 means 'clothes' or 'garments.'
  • 分枣栗与之 - 分 means 'to distribute,' 枣栗 means 'dates and chestnuts,' and 与之 means 'to them' or 'to give to them.'
  • 各餍所欲而往 - 各 means 'each,' 餍 means 'to satisfy,' 所欲 means 'what they desire,' and 而往 means 'and go away.'
  • 分之必均 - 分之 means 'to distribute them,' 必 means 'necessarily,' and 均 means 'equally.'
  • 偶有参差 - 偶有 means 'occasionally,' 参差 means 'uneven' or 'unequal.'
  • 聚而向老人计较 - 聚 means 'to gather,' 而 means 'and,' 向 means 'to,' 老人 means 'elder' or 'wise person,' and 计较 means 'to argue' or 'to discuss.'
  • 尤可爱也 - 尤 means 'especially,' 可爱 means 'lovable,' and 也 is a particle indicating a statement.
  • 余行李半天下 - 余 means 'I,' 行李 means 'traveling' or 'journeying,' 半 means 'half,' and 天下 means 'the world' or 'the entire country.'
  • 所至以客为家 - 所至 means 'wherever I go,' 以 means 'to treat,' 客 means 'guest,' and 为家 means 'as home.'
  • 客两河者 - 客 means 'to be a guest,' 两河 means 'the two rivers' (referring to the Yellow River and the Huai River).
  • 前后十数年 - 前后 means 'before and after,' 十数年 means 'more than ten years.'
  • 始于察荒李御史幕 - 始于 means 'began with,' 察荒 means 'inspecting famine,' 李御史 means 'Mr. Li, the Censor,' and 幕 means 'council' or 'office.'
  • 怀孟薛宗伯知之 - 怀孟 means 'Huai and Meng,' 薛宗伯 means 'Mr. Xue, the Grand Master of Rites,' and 知之 means 'knew about it.'
  • 呼至其家 - 呼 means 'called,' 至 means 'to,' and 其家 means 'his home.'
  • 与仲蒨二兄读书翕园 - 与 means 'together with,' 仲蒨 means 'Mr. Zhong and Mr. Qian,' 二兄 means 'two brothers,' 读书 means 'to study,' and 翕园 means 'Xiyuan Garden.'
  • 后为贾大中丞召修省志 - 后 means 'later,' 为 means 'by,' 贾大中丞 means 'Mr. Jia, the Grand Censor,' 召 means 'called,' 修 means 'to compile,' and 省志 means 'provincial records.'
  • 别去 - 别 means 'to leave,' and 去 means 'to depart.'
  • 越三年 - 越 means 'after,' and 三年 means 'three years.'
  • 会吊宗伯之丧 - 会 means 'happened to,' 吊 means 'to mourn,' 宗伯 means 'Grand Master of Rites,' and 之丧 means 'his funeral.'
  • 黄门卫公先生正在读《礼》 - 黄门 means 'Yellow Gate,' 卫公 means 'Mr. Wei,' 先生 means 'sir' or 'master,' and 正在读《礼》 means 'was reading the Book of Rites.'
  • 留与崥山草堂 - 留 means 'to stay,' 与 means 'with,' and 崥山草堂 means 'Qingshan Cottage.'
  • 商榷今古 - 商榷 means 'to discuss' or 'to deliberate,' and 今古 means 'ancient and modern times.'
  • 又为洛阳太守朱灿煌邀阅试卷 - 又 means 'again,' 为 means 'by,' 洛阳太守 means 'Governor of Luoyang,' 朱灿煌 means 'Mr. Zhu Canhuang,' 邀 means 'invited,' and 阅试卷 means 'to review examination papers.'
  • 别去 - 别 means 'to leave,' and 去 means 'to depart.'
  • 介人之久于兹土者 - 介人 means 'intermediary' or 'middleman,' 之 means 'who,' 久 means 'long,' 于 means 'in,' and 兹土 means 'this land.'
  • 实以宗伯父子恩分滋深 - 实 means 'actually,' 以 means 'because,' 宗伯 means 'Grand Master of Rites,' 父子 means 'father and son,' 恩分 means 'affection,' and 滋深 means 'even deeper.'
  • 故依刘御李 - 故 means 'therefore,' 依 means 'to rely on,' 刘 means 'Liu,' 御 means 'to govern,' and 李 means 'Li.'
  • 马首不能他指耳 - 马首 means 'horse's head,' 不能 means 'cannot,' 他指 means 'point elsewhere,' and 耳 is a particle indicating a statement.
  • 时沈宫詹绎堂先生分巡大梁 - 时 means 'at that time,' 沈宫詹 means 'Mr. Shen, the Palace Censor,' 绎堂 means 'Yetao,' 先生 means 'sir' or 'master,' 分巡 means 'to tour,' and 大梁 means 'Daliang' (another name for Kaifeng).
  • 清慈明允 - 清 means 'clear' or 'pure,' 慈 means 'kind,' 明 means 'bright' or 'wise,' and 允 means 'just.'
  • 为海内岳牧表 - 为 means 'as,' 海内 means 'within the seas' or 'the entire country,' 岳牧 means 'mountain and pasture' (metaphorically referring to leaders), and 表 means 'model' or 'example.'
  • 余驱车八郡 - 余 means 'I,' 驱车 means 'to drive a carriage,' and 八郡 means 'eight prefectures.'
  • 历收河岳之英 - 历 means 'throughout,' 收 means 'to collect,' 河岳 means 'rivers and mountains,' and 之英 means 'the best.'
  • 倦则以钧阳清署为归焉 - 倦 means 'tired,' 则 means 'then,' 以 means 'to treat,' 钧阳 means 'Junyang,' 清署 means 'a clean office,' and 为归焉 means 'as a place to return to.'
  • 其他逆旅主人 - 其他 means 'other,' 逆旅 means 'inn,' and 主人 means 'host.'
  • 无不款暱如戚属 - 无不 means 'all,' 款暱 means 'close and friendly,' and 如戚属 means 'like family.'
  • 水行则戒榜人无妨缓棹 - 水行 means 'traveling by water,' 则 means 'then,' 戒 means 'to instruct,' 榜人 means 'boatman,' 无妨 means 'without hindrance,' and 缓棹 means 'to row slowly.'
  • 柯上逍遥 - 柯 means 'bank' or 'shore,' and 逍遥 means 'to wander freely.'
  • 陆行则常与执辔者试走 - 陆行 means 'traveling by land,' 则 means 'then,' 常 means 'often,' 与 means 'with,' 执辔者 means 'the person holding the reins,' and 试走 means 'to try walking.'

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 殚精音律 - Means 'to exhaust oneself in the study of music theory,' indicating a deep dedication to mastering music.
  • 古今离合之义 - Refers to the principles of music theory that have been established over time, both ancient and modern.
  • 无不博综 - Means 'nothing is not thoroughly understood,' emphasizing comprehensive knowledge.
  • 弦索化工手 - Literally 'master of the strings and plucking,' referring to someone who is highly skilled in playing stringed instruments.
  • 考订音声 - Means 'to examine and correct the sounds,' referring to the process of refining musical performance.
  • 字有讹舛，悉为厘正 - Means 'if there are errors in the notation, they are all corrected,' indicating meticulous attention to detail in musical notation.
  • 八风二十四气 - Refers to the eight winds and twenty-four seasonal changes, a metaphor for the natural harmony that music should reflect.
  • 相为嘘吸 - Means 'to breathe together,' suggesting a harmonious interaction between different elements of music.
  • 海内名公卿 - Refers to famous officials throughout the country, indicating widespread recognition.
  • 文章之士 - Refers to scholars or literati, emphasizing the intellectual community.
  • 名直达禁掖 - Means 'his name reached the inner court,' indicating high status and recognition by the imperial court.
  • 擘阮传人 - Refers to the person who plays the guqin (a type of zither), emphasizing the importance of the instrument in Chinese music.
  • 介人为导师 - Means 'with Jièren as a teacher,' referring to a specific individual who serves as a mentor.
  • 余尝作一想 - Means 'I once had an idea,' introducing a hypothetical scenario.

  • 桓子野《挽歌》 - Refers to Huan Ziye's 'Dirge,' another well-known piece.
  • 孔明《梁父吟》 - Refers to Zhuge Liang's 'Liangfu Ode,' a piece associated with the Three Kingdoms period.
  • 谢安《洛生泳》 - Refers to Xie An's 'Lo-song Ode,' a piece that reflects his influence.

  • 袁山松《行路难》 - Refers to Yuan Shansong's 'Difficult Road,' a piece that reflects the challenges of life.
  • 李太白《乌夜啼》 - Refers to Li Bai's 'Crow Cries at Night,' a piece that reflects his poetic sensibilities.
  • 令相如鼓琴 - Means 'let Sima Xiangru play the qin,' referring to a famous qin player.
  • 桓伊吹玉{遂} - Refers to Huan Yi playing the flute, a specific instrument.
  • 高渐离击筑 - Refers to Gao Jianli playing the zhú, a type of percussion instrument.
  • 祢衡挝渔阳鼓 - Refers to Mi Heng playing the Yuyang drum, another percussion instrument.
  • 君扬出而欹冠短袖 - Means 'Jiuyang came out with a tilted hat and short sleeves,' describing a specific appearance.
  • 为之提掇其间 - Means 'to lead and support in the middle,' suggesting a role in coordinating the performance.
  • 左顾右盼，意气激昂 - Means 'looking left and right, with high spirits,' describing a dynamic and energetic performance.
  • 拨清弦，发哀弄 - Means 'plucking the clear strings, producing mournful tunes,' describing the musical performance.
  • 人声天籁，云委雪飞 - Means 'human voices and the sounds of nature, like clouds and snow,' suggesting a harmonious and beautiful performance.
  • 一洗梨园法曲之陋 - Means 'to wash away the crudeness of the imperial music,' indicating a desire to elevate the quality of music.

Idiomatic Expressions

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 古人名钱曰刀 - Ancients called money 'knife', because it was sharp and could kill.
  • 执两『戈』以求『金』，谓之『钱』 - Holding two '戈' to seek '金', called '钱'.
  • 执两『戈』以求『贝』，谓之『贱』 - Holding two '戈' to seek '贝', called '贱'.
  • 执『十戈』以求『贝』，则谓之『贼』而已矣 - Holding ten '戈' to seek '贝', called '贼' already.
  • 两『戈』不敌一『金』，钱真神物也 - Two '戈' are not enough for one '金', money is truly a divine object.
  • 得一『金』而来两『戈』，岂不可危 - Getting one '金' and two '戈' coming, isn't it dangerous.
  • 操两『戈』以求一『金』，亦复何畏 - Holding two '戈' to seek one '金', what's there to fear.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 汉阳 - A city in Hubei Province, China, known for its historical significance.
  • 汴市 - A market in Bian (modern-day Kaifeng), a city in Henan Province, known for its historical importance.
  • 王介甫 - A reference to Wang Anshi (1021–1086), a prominent statesman and scholar during the Song Dynasty, known for his reforms and literary works.
  • 钱 - The character for 'money' in Chinese, which is composed of '戈' (spear) and '金' (gold), symbolizing the idea that money can be a source of conflict and power.
  • 贱 - The character for 'cheap' or 'lowly', which is composed of '戈' (spear) and '贝' (shell, often used as currency in ancient times), suggesting that seeking cheap things can lead to a lowly status.
  • 贼 - The character for 'thief' or 'traitor', which is composed of '十戈' (ten spears) and '贝' (shell), implying that excessive greed can lead to becoming a thief or traitor.

Proper Nouns

  • 汉阳 - A city in Hubei Province, China.
  • 汴市 - A market in Bian (modern-day Kaifeng), Henan Province.
  • 王介甫 - A reference to Wang Anshi, a prominent statesman and scholar during the Song Dynasty.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 文近万言 - Hyperbolic expression meaning 'the text is nearly ten thousand words long,' emphasizing its length.
  • 读之不厌其长，唯恐其尽 - Idiomatic expression meaning 'one reads it without tiring of its length, only fearing it will end,' highlighting the text's engaging nature.
  • 允称妙构 - Formal expression meaning 'truly deserving of the title of a masterpiece,' praising the text's structure.
  • 予素不识三侬 - Modest expression meaning 'I have never known the Three Nongs,' referring to the author's unfamiliarity with a certain person or group.
  • 令嗣柱东 - Respectful term meaning 'your honorable son, Zudong,' addressing someone's son.
  • 曾通缟紵 - Literary expression meaning 'once exchanged white silk and brocade,' referring to past interactions or exchanges.
  • 因索种种奇书 - Phrase meaning 'therefore sought various strange books,' indicating the pursuit of unusual or rare texts.
  • 尚未惠读 - Modest expression meaning 'have not yet had the privilege of reading,' expressing the speaker's regret for not having read the books.
  • 不知何日方慰予怀也 - Literary expression meaning 'do not know when it will finally console my heart,' expressing anticipation or longing.

  • 金陵为帝王建都之地 - Phrase meaning 'Jinling is the land where emperors established their capital,' highlighting the historical significance of the location.
  • 公侯戚畹，甲第连云 - Literary expression meaning 'princes, marquises, and close relatives of the imperial family, whose mansions stretch up to the clouds,' describing the grandeur of noble residences.
  • 宗室王孙，翩翩裘马 - Phrase meaning 'royal descendants and young nobles, riding in fine furs and horses,' depicting the elegance and wealth of the nobility.
  • 乌衣子弟，湖海宾游 - Literary expression meaning 'the young nobles of the Wu Yi clan and travelers from the lakes and seas,' referring to the elite and their social activities.
  • 挟弹吹箫，经过赵李 - Phrase meaning 'carrying bows and playing flutes, passing through the homes of Zhao and Li,' describing the leisurely and refined activities of the nobility.
  • 每开筵宴 - Phrase meaning 'every time a banquet is held,' setting the scene for social gatherings.
  • 传呼乐籍，罗绮芬芳 - Phrase meaning 'announcing the musicians, with silks and fragrances,' describing the luxurious and pleasant atmosphere of the banquet.
  • 行酒纠觞，留髡送客 - Phrase meaning 'serving wine and managing the cups, keeping the guests entertained until they leave,' depicting the hospitality and entertainment at the banquet.
  • 酒阑棋罢，堕珥遗簪 - Phrase meaning 'when the wine is finished and the games are done, earrings and hairpins are dropped,' describing the relaxed and intimate atmosphere of the gathering.
  • 真欲界之仙都，升平之乐国也 - Literary expression meaning 'truly the capital of desire, the land of peace and joy,' praising the beauty and prosperity of the place.
  • 前门对武定桥，后门在钞库街 - Descriptive phrase meaning 'the front gate faces Wuding Bridge, and the back gate is on Chaokou Street,' providing geographical details.
  • 妓家鳞次，比屋而居 - Phrase meaning 'the houses of courtesans are lined up, one after another,' describing the layout of the area.
  • 屋宇精洁，花木萧疏 - Phrase meaning 'the buildings are clean and neat, with sparse flowers and trees,' describing the appearance of the courtesans' homes.
  • 迥非尘境 - Literary expression meaning 'far from the mundane world,' emphasizing the otherworldly quality of the place.
  • 到门则铜环半启，珠箔低垂 - Phrase meaning 'when you arrive at the door, the copper ring is half open, and the pearl curtains are lowered,' describing the entrance.
  • 升阶则猧儿吠客，鹦哥唤茶 - Phrase meaning 'when you ascend the steps, the little dogs bark at the guests, and the parrots call for tea,' depicting the welcoming atmosphere.

  • 纨绔少年，绣肠才子 - Phrase meaning 'young nobles and talented scholars with embroidered hearts,' referring to the elite and their refined tastes.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 十娘泣曰 - Shi Niang wept and said, where '泣' means weeping, and '曰' is a quotative particle.
  • 君知兒者，何出此言 - Rhetorical question expressing disbelief.
  • 兒雖風塵賤質，然非好淫蕩檢者流 - Contrasts Shi Niang's lowly status with her moral integrity. '風塵' refers to the world of courtesans, and '賤質' means lowly status.
  • 苛兒心之所好，雖相莊如賓，情與之洽也 - Conditional structure describing her feelings towards someone she truly likes. '苛' means 'if' or 'when,' and '相莊如賓' means 'to treat each other with the formality of guests.'
  • 非兒心之所好，恐勉同枕席，不與之合也 - Conditional structure describing her feelings towards someone she does not truly like. '勉' means 'to force' or 'to compel.'
  • 兒之不貞，命也如何 - Rhetorical question expressing her sense of fate. '命也' means 'it is fate' or 'it is destiny.'
  • 言已，涕下沾襟 - After she finished speaking, tears streamed down her cheeks. '言已' means 'after she finished speaking,' and '涕下沾襟' means 'tears wet her sleeves.'
  • 余斂容謝之曰 - I composed myself and apologized, saying. '斂容' means to compose one's face or demeanor, and '謝' means to apologize.
  • 吾失言，吾過矣 - Self-criticism. '吾失言' means 'I spoke wrongly,' and '吾過矣' means 'I was in error.'
  • 嬌啼婉轉，作掌中舞 - She wept softly and gracefully, performing a dance in my palm. '掌中舞' means 'a dance performed in the palm.'

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 歲丙子 - Specific year in the sexagenary cycle, corresponding to 1646 AD (the year of the Ding Si).
  • 盟於眉樓 - Literally 'swore an alliance at Mei Tower,' where '盟' (mén) means to swear an oath or make a pact.
  • 則梁作盟文甚奇 - 'Zeliang composed an oath text that was very strange,' where '甚奇' (shèn qí) means 'very strange' or 'very peculiar.'
  • 姓盟不如臂盟，臂盟不如心盟 - This phrase uses a series of comparisons to emphasize the importance of sincerity in oaths. '姓盟' (xìng méng) refers to an oath by name, '臂盟' (bì méng) refers to an oath by arm, and '心盟' (xīn méng) refers to an oath by heart.
